file.content:
"""One-shot — extract text + seed metadata.json and notes.md for one book.

Reads the PDF locally (faster than fetching from the vault), extracts text
page-by-page with pypdf, then PUTs three files into raw/<book>/:

    text.md         <- extracted text, page-by-page
    metadata.json   <- title, author, pages, ingest date, tags, status
    notes.md        <- empty journal stub for the user

Re-running is safe — every PUT is idempotent. Note that re-running OVERWRITES
notes.md, so don't re-run after you've started writing in it (or guard later).

Run: uv run python extract_book.py
"""

import json
import os
import sys
from datetime import date
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import quote

import requests
import urllib3
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from pypdf import PdfReader

load_dotenv(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / ".env")

API_KEY = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY")
HOST = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_HOST", "127.0.0.1")
PORT = int(os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_PORT", "27124"))
BASE = f"https://{HOST}:{PORT}"

if not API_KEY:
    sys.exit("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY not set in .env")

urllib3.disable_warnings(urllib3.exceptions.InsecureRequestWarning)

session = requests.Session()
session.headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {API_KEY}"
session.verify = False


LOCAL_PDF = Path(
    r"C:\Francisco\OneDrive - UNIVERSIDAD DE INGENIERIA Y TECNOLOGIA"
    r"\learn\books\Mathematics\PUCP\Fundamentos de analisis"
    r"\Analisis Real- Lages.pdf"
)
BOOK_FOLDER = "raw/Analisis Real - Lages"


def put_text(path: str, body: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body.encode("utf-8"),
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=60,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def put_json(path: str, body: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body.encode("utf-8"),
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
        timeout=60,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


if not LOCAL_PDF.exists():
    sys.exit(f"PDF not found: {LOCAL_PDF}")

reader = PdfReader(LOCAL_PDF)
n_pages = len(reader.pages)
print(f"Source:    {LOCAL_PDF.name}")
print(f"Pages:     {n_pages}")

# --- text.md
parts = [f"# Analisis Real - Lages\n\nText extracted from `source.pdf` ({n_pages} pages).\n"]
empty_pages = 0
for i, page in enumerate(reader.pages, 1):
    txt = (page.extract_text() or "").strip()
    if not txt:
        empty_pages += 1
    parts.append(f"\n## Page {i}\n\n{txt}\n")
text_md = "\n".join(parts)
print(f"Extracted: {len(text_md):,} chars  ({empty_pages} empty pages)")

# --- metadata.json
m = reader.metadata or {}
metadata = {
    "title": str(m.get("/Title") or "Análisis Real"),
    "author": str(m.get("/Author") or "Elon Lages Lima"),
    "pages": n_pages,
    "language": "es",
    "course": "Fundamentos de Análisis",
    "institution": "PUCP",
    "source_path": str(LOCAL_PDF),
    "ingested_at": date.today().isoformat(),
    "tags": ["math", "real-analysis", "PUCP", "spanish"],
    "status": "reading",
    "rating": None,
}

# --- notes.md (append-only journal stub — only written if it doesn't exist)
notes_md = f"""# Notes — Análisis Real (Lages)

Append-only dated journal. Newest entries at the bottom.

Each entry: `## YYYY-MM-DD — ch. N §M` then prose. The compile pass folds
these into wiki/concepts/* and wiki/books/Analisis Real - Lages.md.

---

## {date.today().isoformat()} — initial ingest

(write your first thoughts here)
"""

# Skip notes.md if it already has content (don't clobber the user's writing)
existing = session.get(
    f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(f'{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md')}",
    headers={"Accept": "text/markdown"},
    timeout=10,
)
notes_exists = existing.status_code == 200 and existing.text.strip() != notes_md.strip()

print()
print(f"PUT /vault/{BOOK_FOLDER}/text.md       -> {put_text(f'{BOOK_FOLDER}/text.md', text_md)}")
print(f"PUT /vault/{BOOK_FOLDER}/metadata.json -> {put_json(f'{BOOK_FOLDER}/metadata.json', json.dumps(metadata, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))}")
if notes_exists:
    print(f"SKIP /vault/{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md       (already has user content)")
else:
    print(f"PUT /vault/{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md      -> {put_text(f'{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md', notes_md)}")

"""One-shot — extract text + seed metadata.json, notes.md, and a wiki stub.

Reads the PDF locally (faster than fetching from the vault), extracts text
page-by-page with pypdf, then PUTs four files:

    raw/<book>/text.md             <- extracted text, page-by-page
    raw/<book>/metadata.json       <- title, author, pages, ingest date
    raw/<book>/notes.md            <- empty journal stub for the user
    wiki/books/<book>.md           <- stub hub page so the book is graph-connected

text.md and notes.md each include a navigation callout linking to the wiki
hub + each other, so the per-book subgraph is wired from ingest.

notes.md is overwritten only if the placeholder string is still present
(i.e. the user hasn't edited it yet).

Run: uv run python extract_book.py
"""

import json
import os
import sys
from datetime import date
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import quote

import requests
import urllib3
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from pypdf import PdfReader

load_dotenv(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / ".env")

API_KEY = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY")
HOST = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_HOST", "127.0.0.1")
PORT = int(os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_PORT", "27124"))
BASE = f"https://{HOST}:{PORT}"

if not API_KEY:
    sys.exit("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY not set in .env")

urllib3.disable_warnings(urllib3.exceptions.InsecureRequestWarning)

session = requests.Session()
session.headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {API_KEY}"
session.verify = False


LOCAL_PDF = Path(
    r"C:\Francisco\OneDrive - UNIVERSIDAD DE INGENIERIA Y TECNOLOGIA"
    r"\learn\books\Mathematics\PUCP\Fundamentos de analisis"
    r"\Analisis Real- Lages.pdf"
)
BOOK_FOLDER = "raw/Analisis Real - Lages"


def put_text(path: str, body: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body.encode("utf-8"),
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=60,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def put_json(path: str, body: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body.encode("utf-8"),
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
        timeout=60,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


if not LOCAL_PDF.exists():
    sys.exit(f"PDF not found: {LOCAL_PDF}")

reader = PdfReader(LOCAL_PDF)
n_pages = len(reader.pages)
print(f"Source:    {LOCAL_PDF.name}")
print(f"Pages:     {n_pages}")

# --- text.md
parts = [f"# Analisis Real - Lages\n\nText extracted from `source.pdf` ({n_pages} pages).\n"]
empty_pages = 0
for i, page in enumerate(reader.pages, 1):
    txt = (page.extract_text() or "").strip()
    if not txt:
        empty_pages += 1
    parts.append(f"\n## Page {i}\n\n{txt}\n")
text_md = "\n".join(parts)
print(f"Extracted: {len(text_md):,} chars  ({empty_pages} empty pages)")

# --- metadata.json
m = reader.metadata or {}
metadata = {
    "title": str(m.get("/Title") or "Análisis Real"),
    "author": str(m.get("/Author") or "Elon Lages Lima"),
    "pages": n_pages,
    "language": "es",
    "course": "Fundamentos de Análisis",
    "institution": "PUCP",
    "source_path": str(LOCAL_PDF),
    "ingested_at": date.today().isoformat(),
    "tags": ["math", "real-analysis", "PUCP", "spanish"],
    "status": "reading",
    "rating": None,
}

# --- notes.md (append-only journal stub — only written if it doesn't exist)
notes_md = f"""# Notes — Análisis Real (Lages)

Append-only dated journal. Newest entries at the bottom.

Each entry: `## YYYY-MM-DD — ch. N §M` then prose. The compile pass folds
these into wiki/concepts/* and wiki/books/Analisis Real - Lages.md.

---

## {date.today().isoformat()} — initial ingest

(write your first thoughts here)
"""

# Skip notes.md if it already has content (don't clobber the user's writing)
existing = session.get(
    f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(f'{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md')}",
    headers={"Accept": "text/markdown"},
    timeout=10,
)
notes_exists = existing.status_code == 200 and existing.text.strip() != notes_md.strip()

print()
print(f"PUT /vault/{BOOK_FOLDER}/text.md       -> {put_text(f'{BOOK_FOLDER}/text.md', text_md)}")
print(f"PUT /vault/{BOOK_FOLDER}/metadata.json -> {put_json(f'{BOOK_FOLDER}/metadata.json', json.dumps(metadata, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))}")
if notes_exists:
    print(f"SKIP /vault/{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md       (already has user content)")
else:
    print(f"PUT /vault/{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md      -> {put_text(f'{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md', notes_md)}")

WIKI_PAGE = f"wiki/books/{BOOK_NAME}.md"

NOTES_PLACEHOLDER = "(write your first thoughts here)"


def nav_block(extra: str = "") -> str:
    """Obsidian collapsible callout — same in text.md and notes.md."""
    lines = [
        f"> [!info]- {BOOK_NAME} — navigation",
        f"> - **Wiki:** [[{BOOK_NAME}]]",
        f"> - **Notes:** [[{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes|notes]]",
        f"> - **Text:** [[{BOOK_FOLDER}/text|extracted text]]",
        f"> - **PDF:** [[{BOOK_FOLDER}/source.pdf|source.pdf]]",
    ]
    if extra:
        lines.append(f"> - {extra}")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def put_text(path: str, body: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body.encode("utf-8"),
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=60,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def put_json(path: str, body: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body.encode("utf-8"),
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
        timeout=60,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


if not LOCAL_PDF.exists():
    sys.exit(f"PDF not found: {LOCAL_PDF}")

reader = PdfReader(LOCAL_PDF)
n_pages = len(reader.pages)
print(f"Source:    {LOCAL_PDF.name}")
print(f"Pages:     {n_pages}")

# --- text.md
parts = [
    f"# {BOOK_NAME} — extracted text\n",
    nav_block(f"**Pages:** {n_pages}"),
    f"\nText extracted from `source.pdf` ({n_pages} pages).\n",
]
empty_pages = 0
for i, page in enumerate(reader.pages, 1):
    txt = (page.extract_text() or "").strip()
    if not txt:
        empty_pages += 1
    parts.append(f"\n## Page {i}\n\n{txt}\n")
text_md = "\n".join(parts)
print(f"Extracted: {len(text_md):,} chars  ({empty_pages} empty pages)")

# --- metadata.json
m = reader.metadata or {}
metadata = {
    "title": str(m.get("/Title") or "Análisis Real"),
    "author": str(m.get("/Author") or "Elon Lages Lima"),
    "pages": n_pages,
    "language": "es",
    "course": "Fundamentos de Análisis",
    "institution": "PUCP",
    "source_path": str(LOCAL_PDF),
    "ingested_at": date.today().isoformat(),
    "tags": ["math", "real-analysis", "PUCP", "spanish"],
    "status": "reading",
    "rating": None,
}

# --- notes.md (append-only journal stub — only written if it doesn't exist)
notes_md = f"""# Notes — Análisis Real (Lages)

Append-only dated journal. Newest entries at the bottom.

Each entry: `## YYYY-MM-DD — ch. N §M` then prose. The compile pass folds
these into wiki/concepts/* and wiki/books/Analisis Real - Lages.md.

---

## {date.today().isoformat()} — initial ingest

(write your first thoughts here)
"""

# Skip notes.md if it already has content (don't clobber the user's writing)
existing = session.get(
    f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(f'{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md')}",
    headers={"Accept": "text/markdown"},
    timeout=10,
)
notes_exists = existing.status_code == 200 and existing.text.strip() != notes_md.strip()

print()
print(f"PUT /vault/{BOOK_FOLDER}/text.md       -> {put_text(f'{BOOK_FOLDER}/text.md', text_md)}")
print(f"PUT /vault/{BOOK_FOLDER}/metadata.json -> {put_json(f'{BOOK_FOLDER}/metadata.json', json.dumps(metadata, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))}")
if notes_exists:
    print(f"SKIP /vault/{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md       (already has user content)")
else:
    print(f"PUT /vault/{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md      -> {put_text(f'{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md', notes_md)}")

aiTitle: Index Obsidian API and add to nia.md

content.text:
Two separate things going on:

**The PDF doesn't show in the graph by default — that's expected.** Obsidian's graph view only renders `.md` files. PDFs, JSON, and images are filtered out unless you toggle "Attachments" on in the graph filter panel — but PDFs can't contain wikilinks, so even when shown they appear as isolated nodes with no edges. The connections you're seeing (text ↔ notes ↔ Analisis Real - Lages) are correct and complete for what Obsidian can show. The link from text/notes → `source.pdf` exists in the markdown; it just doesn't render in the graph.

**The READMEs are clutter from my initial scaffolding.** I created `raw/README.md` + 4× `wiki/<section>/README.md` as folder-explainer pages, and wired the wiki ones into `wiki/index.md`. They were useful as orientation docs but have no role in the actual KB — they just create a 5-node "README" cluster around `index`. The orientation content already lives in our conversation; no reason to keep stub pages in the vault.

I'll delete all 5 READMEs and rewrite `wiki/index.md` to link to actual content (the Lages book + the ghost author/topic pages it references). One-shot cleanup script.

input.content:
"""One-shot — drop the 5 placeholder README pages and rewrite wiki/index.md.

The READMEs were scaffolding from the initial layout setup. They show up in
the graph view as a 5-node cluster with no real meaning. Deleting them and
pointing wiki/index.md at actual content makes the graph honest.

Re-running is safe — DELETE 404s and PUT is idempotent.

Run: uv run python cleanup_readmes.py
"""

import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import quote

import requests
import urllib3
from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / ".env")

API_KEY = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY")
HOST = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_HOST", "127.0.0.1")
PORT = int(os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_PORT", "27124"))
BASE = f"https://{HOST}:{PORT}"

if not API_KEY:
    sys.exit("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY not set in .env")

urllib3.disable_warnings(urllib3.exceptions.InsecureRequestWarning)

session = requests.Session()
session.headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {API_KEY}"
session.verify = False


TO_DELETE = [
    "raw/README.md",
    "wiki/books/README.md",
    "wiki/concepts/README.md",
    "wiki/authors/README.md",
    "wiki/topics/README.md",
]

INDEX = """# Knowledge base — index

Top-level map of content. Maintained by the LLM on compile passes.

## Books

- [[Analisis Real - Lages]]

## Concepts

_(empty — populated by compile pass from each book's text + notes)_

## Authors

- [[Elon Lages Lima]]

## Topics

- [[Real analysis]]
"""


for path in TO_DELETE:
    r = session.delete(f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}", timeout=10)
    print(f"DELETE /vault/{path:40s} -> {r.status_code}")

r = session.put(
    f"{BASE}/vault/{quote('wiki/index.md')}",
    data=INDEX.encode("utf-8"),
    headers={"Content-Type": "text/markdown"},
    timeout=10,
)
r.raise_for_status()
print(f"PUT    /vault/wiki/index.md{' ' * 28}-> {r.status_code}")

import os, urllib3, requests
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import quote
from dotenv import load_dotenv

BASE = 'https://127.0.0.1:27124'

def walk(path=''):
    out = []
    for e in s.get(f'{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}', timeout=5).json()['files']:
        full = f'{path}{e}'
        if e.endswith('/'): out.extend(walk(full))
        else: out.append(full)
    return out

for p in walk(): print(p)

aiTitle: Index Obsidian API and add to nia.md

content.text:
Two genuine architecture issues with `raw/`:

1. **`metadata.json` is orphaned** because Obsidian wikilinks don't reach into JSON. The Obsidian-native fix is **YAML frontmatter** instead of a sidecar file — properties become queryable (Dataview), the data attaches to a real graph node, and no orphan appears. Metadata describes the *book*, so it lives on `wiki/books/<book>.md` (the hub). `text.md` and `notes.md` get a minimal `book:` field to group artifacts.

2. **`text.md` is one 392K-char wall**. The PDF has an outline — let's chunk by chapter into `text/<NN> - <Chapter>.md` so each chapter is a graph node, and the LLM can pull a single chapter without lugging 230 unrelated pages.

input.content:
"""One-shot — extract text + write the per-book layout to the vault.

Layout (book-folder-as-self-contained):

    raw/<book>/
      <book>.md           <- HUB: YAML frontmatter + summary stub + nav
      text.md             <- extracted text (regenerable, page-by-page)
      notes.md            <- your journal (only seeded if untouched)
      source.pdf          <- already uploaded by ingest_pdf.py

The hub lives next to the raw artifacts so each book is one self-contained
folder. wiki/ stays purely cross-cutting (concepts, authors, topics).

YAML frontmatter replaces the old metadata.json — Obsidian renders it as
Properties in the side panel and Dataview queries it directly. No orphan
JSON node in the graph.

Re-runnable. Cleans up legacy files (metadata.json, wiki/books/<book>.md)
on the first run. notes.md is overwritten only while the placeholder
string is still present.

Run: uv run python extract_book.py
"""

import json
import os
import sys
from datetime import date
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import quote

import requests
import urllib3
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from pypdf import PdfReader

load_dotenv(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / ".env")

API_KEY = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY")
HOST = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_HOST", "127.0.0.1")
PORT = int(os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_PORT", "27124"))
BASE = f"https://{HOST}:{PORT}"

if not API_KEY:
    sys.exit("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY not set in .env")

urllib3.disable_warnings(urllib3.exceptions.InsecureRequestWarning)

session = requests.Session()
session.headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {API_KEY}"
session.verify = False


LOCAL_PDF = Path(
    r"C:\Francisco\OneDrive - UNIVERSIDAD DE INGENIERIA Y TECNOLOGIA"
    r"\learn\books\Mathematics\PUCP\Fundamentos de analisis"
    r"\Analisis Real- Lages.pdf"
)
BOOK_NAME = "Analisis Real - Lages"
BOOK_FOLDER = f"raw/{BOOK_NAME}"
HUB_PATH = f"{BOOK_FOLDER}/{BOOK_NAME}.md"
TEXT_PATH = f"{BOOK_FOLDER}/text.md"
NOTES_PATH = f"{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md"

NOTES_PLACEHOLDER = "(write your first thoughts here)"

LEGACY_PATHS = [
    f"{BOOK_FOLDER}/metadata.json",
    f"wiki/books/{BOOK_NAME}.md",
]


def put_text(path: str, body: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body.encode("utf-8"),
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=60,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def delete_path(path: str) -> int:
    r = session.delete(f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}", timeout=10)
    return r.status_code


def has_user_content(path: str, placeholder: str) -> bool:
    """True if the file exists AND no longer contains the placeholder."""
    r = session.get(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        headers={"Accept": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=10,
    )
    return r.status_code == 200 and placeholder not in r.text


def yaml_str(v) -> str:
    """Quote a value for YAML — strings with special chars get double-quoted."""
    if v is None:
        return "null"
    if isinstance(v, bool):
        return "true" if v else "false"
    if isinstance(v, (int, float)):
        return str(v)
    if isinstance(v, list):
        return "[" + ", ".join(yaml_str(x) for x in v) + "]"
    s = str(v)
    return json.dumps(s, ensure_ascii=False)


def yaml_block(d: dict) -> str:
    lines = ["---"]
    for k, v in d.items():
        lines.append(f"{k}: {yaml_str(v)}")
    lines.append("---")
    return "\n".join(lines)


if not LOCAL_PDF.exists():
    sys.exit(f"PDF not found: {LOCAL_PDF}")

reader = PdfReader(LOCAL_PDF)
n_pages = len(reader.pages)
print(f"Source:    {LOCAL_PDF.name}")
print(f"Pages:     {n_pages}")

m = reader.metadata or {}
book_props = {
    "title": str(m.get("/Title") or "Análisis Real"),
    "author": str(m.get("/Author") or "Elon Lages Lima"),
    "pages": n_pages,
    "language": "es",
    "course": "Fundamentos de Análisis",
    "institution": "PUCP",
    "source_path": str(LOCAL_PDF),
    "ingested_at": date.today().isoformat(),
    "tags": ["math", "real-analysis", "PUCP", "spanish"],
    "status": "reading",
    "rating": None,
}


def nav(extra: str = "") -> str:
    lines = [
        f"> [!info]- {BOOK_NAME} — navigation",
        f"> - **Book:** [[{BOOK_NAME}]]",
        f"> - **Text:** [[{BOOK_FOLDER}/text|extracted text]]",
        f"> - **Notes:** [[{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes|notes]]",
        f"> - **PDF:** [[{BOOK_FOLDER}/source.pdf|source.pdf]]",
    ]
    if extra:
        lines.append(f"> - {extra}")
    return "\n".join(lines)


# --- hub: raw/<book>/<book>.md
hub_md = f"""{yaml_block(book_props)}

# {BOOK_NAME}

{nav()}

> [!warning]- Stub summary
> Placeholder. The compile pass replaces this with a chapter-by-chapter
> summary, key concepts, theorems, and exercises worth solving.

## Author

- [[{book_props["author"]}]]

## Topics

- [[Real analysis]]
"""


# --- text.md
text_props = {
    "book": f"[[{BOOK_NAME}]]",
    "type": "extracted-text",
    "pages": n_pages,
}
parts = [
    yaml_block(text_props),
    "",
    f"# {BOOK_NAME} — extracted text",
    "",
    nav(f"**Pages:** {n_pages}"),
    "",
    f"Text extracted from `source.pdf` ({n_pages} pages).",
]
empty_pages = 0
for i, page in enumerate(reader.pages, 1):
    txt = (page.extract_text() or "").strip()
    if not txt:
        empty_pages += 1
    parts.append(f"\n## Page {i}\n\n{txt}\n")
text_md = "\n".join(parts)
print(f"Extracted: {len(text_md):,} chars  ({empty_pages} empty pages)")


# --- notes.md
notes_props = {
    "book": f"[[{BOOK_NAME}]]",
    "type": "reading-notes",
}
notes_md = f"""{yaml_block(notes_props)}

# Notes — {BOOK_NAME}

{nav()}

Append-only dated journal. Newest entries at the bottom.

Each entry: `## YYYY-MM-DD — ch. N §M` then prose. The compile pass folds
these into `wiki/concepts/*` and the book hub.

---

## {date.today().isoformat()} — initial ingest

{NOTES_PLACEHOLDER}
"""


# --- write
print()
print(f"PUT    /vault/{HUB_PATH:50s} -> {put_text(HUB_PATH, hub_md)}")
print(f"PUT    /vault/{TEXT_PATH:50s} -> {put_text(TEXT_PATH, text_md)}")
if has_user_content(NOTES_PATH, NOTES_PLACEHOLDER):
    print(f"SKIP   /vault/{NOTES_PATH:50s} (you have written notes)")
else:
    print(f"PUT    /vault/{NOTES_PATH:50s} -> {put_text(NOTES_PATH, notes_md)}")

# --- legacy cleanup (idempotent)
for legacy in LEGACY_PATHS:
    code = delete_path(legacy)
    print(f"DELETE /vault/{legacy:50s} -> {code}")

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""One-shot — extract text + seed metadata.json, notes.md, and a wiki stub.

Reads the PDF locally (faster than fetching from the vault), extracts text
page-by-page with pypdf, then PUTs four files:

    raw/<book>/text.md             <- extracted text, page-by-page
    raw/<book>/metadata.json       <- title, author, pages, ingest date
    raw/<book>/notes.md            <- empty journal stub for the user
    wiki/books/<book>.md           <- stub hub page so the book is graph-connected

text.md and notes.md each include a navigation callout linking to the wiki
hub + each other, so the per-book subgraph is wired from ingest.

notes.md is overwritten only if the placeholder string is still present
(i.e. the user hasn't edited it yet).

Run: uv run python extract_book.py
"""

import json
import os
import sys
from datetime import date
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import quote

import requests
import urllib3
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from pypdf import PdfReader

load_dotenv(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / ".env")

API_KEY = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY")
HOST = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_HOST", "127.0.0.1")
PORT = int(os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_PORT", "27124"))
BASE = f"https://{HOST}:{PORT}"

if not API_KEY:
    sys.exit("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY not set in .env")

urllib3.disable_warnings(urllib3.exceptions.InsecureRequestWarning)

session = requests.Session()
session.headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {API_KEY}"
session.verify = False


LOCAL_PDF = Path(
    r"C:\Francisco\OneDrive - UNIVERSIDAD DE INGENIERIA Y TECNOLOGIA"
    r"\learn\books\Mathematics\PUCP\Fundamentos de analisis"
    r"\Analisis Real- Lages.pdf"
)
BOOK_NAME = "Analisis Real - Lages"
BOOK_FOLDER = f"raw/{BOOK_NAME}"
WIKI_PAGE = f"wiki/books/{BOOK_NAME}.md"

NOTES_PLACEHOLDER = "(write your first thoughts here)"


def nav_block(extra: str = "") -> str:
    """Obsidian collapsible callout — same in text.md and notes.md."""
    lines = [
        f"> [!info]- {BOOK_NAME} — navigation",
        f"> - **Wiki:** [[{BOOK_NAME}]]",
        f"> - **Notes:** [[{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes|notes]]",
        f"> - **Text:** [[{BOOK_FOLDER}/text|extracted text]]",
        f"> - **PDF:** [[{BOOK_FOLDER}/source.pdf|source.pdf]]",
    ]
    if extra:
        lines.append(f"> - {extra}")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def put_text(path: str, body: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body.encode("utf-8"),
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=60,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def put_json(path: str, body: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body.encode("utf-8"),
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
        timeout=60,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


if not LOCAL_PDF.exists():
    sys.exit(f"PDF not found: {LOCAL_PDF}")

reader = PdfReader(LOCAL_PDF)
n_pages = len(reader.pages)
print(f"Source:    {LOCAL_PDF.name}")
print(f"Pages:     {n_pages}")

# --- text.md
parts = [
    f"# {BOOK_NAME} — extracted text\n",
    nav_block(f"**Pages:** {n_pages}"),
    f"\nText extracted from `source.pdf` ({n_pages} pages).\n",
]
empty_pages = 0
for i, page in enumerate(reader.pages, 1):
    txt = (page.extract_text() or "").strip()
    if not txt:
        empty_pages += 1
    parts.append(f"\n## Page {i}\n\n{txt}\n")
text_md = "\n".join(parts)
print(f"Extracted: {len(text_md):,} chars  ({empty_pages} empty pages)")

# --- metadata.json
m = reader.metadata or {}
metadata = {
    "title": str(m.get("/Title") or "Análisis Real"),
    "author": str(m.get("/Author") or "Elon Lages Lima"),
    "pages": n_pages,
    "language": "es",
    "course": "Fundamentos de Análisis",
    "institution": "PUCP",
    "source_path": str(LOCAL_PDF),
    "ingested_at": date.today().isoformat(),
    "tags": ["math", "real-analysis", "PUCP", "spanish"],
    "status": "reading",
    "rating": None,
}

# --- notes.md (append-only journal stub — overwritten only if untouched)
notes_md = f"""# Notes — {BOOK_NAME}

{nav_block()}

Append-only dated journal. Newest entries at the bottom.

Each entry: `## YYYY-MM-DD — ch. N §M` then prose. The compile pass folds
these into `wiki/concepts/*` and `wiki/books/{BOOK_NAME}.md`.

---

## {date.today().isoformat()} — initial ingest

{NOTES_PLACEHOLDER}
"""

> [!warning]- Stub page

## Sources

- [[{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes|Reading notes (your journal)]]
- [[{BOOK_FOLDER}/source.pdf|Original PDF]]
- Metadata: `{BOOK_FOLDER}/metadata.json`

## Author

- [[{metadata['author']}]]

## Topics

- [[Real analysis]]
"""


def has_user_content(path: str, placeholder: str) -> bool:
    """True if the file exists AND no longer contains the placeholder."""
    r = session.get(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        headers={"Accept": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=10,
    )
    return r.status_code == 200 and placeholder not in r.text


print()
print(f"PUT /vault/{BOOK_FOLDER}/text.md       -> {put_text(f'{BOOK_FOLDER}/text.md', text_md)}")
print(f"PUT /vault/{BOOK_FOLDER}/metadata.json -> {put_json(f'{BOOK_FOLDER}/metadata.json', json.dumps(metadata, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))}")
if has_user_content(f"{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md", NOTES_PLACEHOLDER):
    print(f"SKIP /vault/{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md       (you have written notes)")
else:
    print(f"PUT /vault/{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md      -> {put_text(f'{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md', notes_md)}")
print(f"PUT /vault/{WIKI_PAGE}  -> {put_text(WIKI_PAGE, wiki_md)}")

import os, urllib3, requests
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import quote
from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv(Path('.').resolve().parent.parent / '.env')
urllib3.disable_warnings(urllib3.exceptions.InsecureRequestWarning)
s = requests.Session()
s.headers['Authorization'] = f'Bearer {os.getenv(\"OBSIDIAN_API_KEY\")}'
s.verify = False
BASE = 'https://127.0.0.1:27124'

def walk(path=''):
    out = []
    for e in s.get(f'{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}', timeout=5).json()['files']:
        full = f'{path}{e}'
        if e.endswith('/'): out.extend(walk(full))
        else: out.append(full)
    return out

for p in walk(): print(p)
"

content.text:
`raw/` architecture is now solid. The new state of your vault:

```
Elon Lages Lima.md                                  <- from clicking the ghost link in graph view
raw/Analisis Real - Lages/
  Analisis Real - Lages.md   <- HUB (frontmatter: title/author/pages/status/tags + summary stub + nav)
  text.md                     <- frontmatter: book + type, then page-by-page content
  notes.md                    <- frontmatter: book + type, then your journal
  source.pdf
```

What changed architecturally:

- **`metadata.json` is gone** — every field moved into YAML frontmatter on the hub page. Open the hub in Obsidian and you'll see Properties in the side panel (title, author, pages, course, tags, status, rating). Dataview queries like `TABLE author, status FROM "raw"` now work.
- **The hub IS the title file** (`Analisis Real - Lages/Analisis Real - Lages.md`) — one central node per book, living next to its raw artifacts.
- **`text.md` and `notes.md` got `book: "[[Analisis Real - Lages]]"` frontmatter** — Dataview can group all artifacts of a book.
- **`wiki/books/` is gone** — that layer was redundant. `wiki/` will only hold cross-book stuff (concepts, authors, topics).

Two things to know:

1. The empty `Elon Lages Lima.md` at the root came from you clicking the ghost link in graph view. Obsidian creates new notes at the vault root by default. The compile pass would normally relocate it to `wiki/authors/Elon Lages Lima.md`. I'll handle this when we do that pass.
2. `wiki/index.md` is also gone (you may have deleted the whole `wiki/` folder, or it got swept). I haven't recreated it since you said focus on raw — let me know if you want a top-level MOC at `wiki/index.md` regenerated, or if we just lean on the hub-page-per-book pattern for now.

Refresh Obsidian — the hub page should now show Properties in the right panel, and the graph node `Analisis Real - Lages` lives inside the book's folder instead of in `wiki/books/`.

"""One-shot — extract text + write the per-book layout to the vault.

Layout (book-folder-as-self-contained):

    raw/<book>/
      <book>.md           <- HUB: YAML frontmatter + summary stub + nav
      text.md             <- extracted text (regenerable, page-by-page)
      notes.md            <- your journal (only seeded if untouched)
      source.pdf          <- already uploaded by ingest_pdf.py

The hub lives next to the raw artifacts so each book is one self-contained
folder. wiki/ stays purely cross-cutting (concepts, authors, topics).

YAML frontmatter replaces the old metadata.json — Obsidian renders it as
Properties in the side panel and Dataview queries it directly. No orphan
JSON node in the graph.

Re-runnable. Cleans up legacy files (metadata.json, wiki/books/<book>.md)
on the first run. notes.md is overwritten only while the placeholder
string is still present.

Run: uv run python extract_book.py
"""

import json
import os
import sys
from datetime import date
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import quote

import requests
import urllib3
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from pypdf import PdfReader

load_dotenv(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / ".env")

API_KEY = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY")
HOST = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_HOST", "127.0.0.1")
PORT = int(os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_PORT", "27124"))
BASE = f"https://{HOST}:{PORT}"

if not API_KEY:
    sys.exit("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY not set in .env")

urllib3.disable_warnings(urllib3.exceptions.InsecureRequestWarning)

session = requests.Session()
session.headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {API_KEY}"
session.verify = False


LOCAL_PDF = Path(
    r"C:\Francisco\OneDrive - UNIVERSIDAD DE INGENIERIA Y TECNOLOGIA"
    r"\learn\books\Mathematics\PUCP\Fundamentos de analisis"
    r"\Analisis Real- Lages.pdf"
)
BOOK_NAME = "Analisis Real - Lages"
BOOK_FOLDER = f"raw/{BOOK_NAME}"
HUB_PATH = f"{BOOK_FOLDER}/{BOOK_NAME}.md"
TEXT_PATH = f"{BOOK_FOLDER}/text.md"
NOTES_PATH = f"{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md"

NOTES_PLACEHOLDER = "(write your first thoughts here)"

LEGACY_PATHS = [
    f"{BOOK_FOLDER}/metadata.json",
    f"wiki/books/{BOOK_NAME}.md",
]


def put_text(path: str, body: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body.encode("utf-8"),
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=60,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def delete_path(path: str) -> int:
    r = session.delete(f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}", timeout=10)
    return r.status_code


def has_user_content(path: str, placeholder: str) -> bool:
    """True if the file exists AND no longer contains the placeholder."""
    r = session.get(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        headers={"Accept": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=10,
    )
    return r.status_code == 200 and placeholder not in r.text


def yaml_str(v) -> str:
    """Quote a value for YAML — strings with special chars get double-quoted."""
    if v is None:
        return "null"
    if isinstance(v, bool):
        return "true" if v else "false"
    if isinstance(v, (int, float)):
        return str(v)
    if isinstance(v, list):
        return "[" + ", ".join(yaml_str(x) for x in v) + "]"
    s = str(v)
    return json.dumps(s, ensure_ascii=False)


def yaml_block(d: dict) -> str:
    lines = ["---"]
    for k, v in d.items():
        lines.append(f"{k}: {yaml_str(v)}")
    lines.append("---")
    return "\n".join(lines)


if not LOCAL_PDF.exists():
    sys.exit(f"PDF not found: {LOCAL_PDF}")

reader = PdfReader(LOCAL_PDF)
n_pages = len(reader.pages)
print(f"Source:    {LOCAL_PDF.name}")
print(f"Pages:     {n_pages}")

m = reader.metadata or {}
book_props = {
    "title": str(m.get("/Title") or "Análisis Real"),
    "author": str(m.get("/Author") or "Elon Lages Lima"),
    "pages": n_pages,
    "language": "es",
    "course": "Fundamentos de Análisis",
    "institution": "PUCP",
    "source_path": str(LOCAL_PDF),
    "ingested_at": date.today().isoformat(),
    "tags": ["math", "real-analysis", "PUCP", "spanish"],
    "status": "reading",
    "rating": None,
}


def nav(extra: str = "") -> str:
    lines = [
        f"> [!info]- {BOOK_NAME} — navigation",
        f"> - **Book:** [[{BOOK_NAME}]]",
        f"> - **Text:** [[{BOOK_FOLDER}/text|extracted text]]",
        f"> - **Notes:** [[{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes|notes]]",
        f"> - **PDF:** [[{BOOK_FOLDER}/source.pdf|source.pdf]]",
    ]
    if extra:
        lines.append(f"> - {extra}")
    return "\n".join(lines)


# --- hub: raw/<book>/<book>.md
hub_md = f"""{yaml_block(book_props)}

# {BOOK_NAME}

{nav()}

> [!warning]- Stub summary
> Placeholder. The compile pass replaces this with a chapter-by-chapter
> summary, key concepts, theorems, and exercises worth solving.

## Author

- [[{book_props["author"]}]]

## Topics

- [[Real analysis]]
"""


# --- text.md
text_props = {
    "book": f"[[{BOOK_NAME}]]",
    "type": "extracted-text",
    "pages": n_pages,
}
parts = [
    yaml_block(text_props),
    "",
    f"# {BOOK_NAME} — extracted text",
    "",
    nav(f"**Pages:** {n_pages}"),
    "",
    f"Text extracted from `source.pdf` ({n_pages} pages).",
]
empty_pages = 0
for i, page in enumerate(reader.pages, 1):
    txt = (page.extract_text() or "").strip()
    if not txt:
        empty_pages += 1
    parts.append(f"\n## Page {i}\n\n{txt}\n")
text_md = "\n".join(parts)
print(f"Extracted: {len(text_md):,} chars  ({empty_pages} empty pages)")


# --- notes.md
notes_props = {
    "book": f"[[{BOOK_NAME}]]",
    "type": "reading-notes",
}
notes_md = f"""{yaml_block(notes_props)}

# Notes — {BOOK_NAME}

{nav()}

Append-only dated journal. Newest entries at the bottom.

Each entry: `## YYYY-MM-DD — ch. N §M` then prose. The compile pass folds
these into `wiki/concepts/*` and the book hub.

---

## {date.today().isoformat()} — initial ingest

{NOTES_PLACEHOLDER}
"""


# --- write
print()
print(f"PUT    /vault/{HUB_PATH:50s} -> {put_text(HUB_PATH, hub_md)}")
print(f"PUT    /vault/{TEXT_PATH:50s} -> {put_text(TEXT_PATH, text_md)}")
if has_user_content(NOTES_PATH, NOTES_PLACEHOLDER):
    print(f"SKIP   /vault/{NOTES_PATH:50s} (you have written notes)")
else:
    print(f"PUT    /vault/{NOTES_PATH:50s} -> {put_text(NOTES_PATH, notes_md)}")

# --- legacy cleanup (idempotent)
for legacy in LEGACY_PATHS:
    code = delete_path(legacy)
    print(f"DELETE /vault/{legacy:50s} -> {code}")

> summary, key concepts, theorems, and exercises worth solving.
"""


# --- text.md
text_props = {
    "book": f"[[{BOOK_NAME}]]",
    "type": "extracted-text",
    "pages": n_pages,
}
parts = [
    yaml_block(text_props),
    "",
    f"# {BOOK_NAME} — extracted text",
    "",
    nav(f"**Pages:** {n_pages}"),
    "",
    f"Text extracted from `source.pdf` ({n_pages} pages).",
]
empty_pages = 0
for i, page in enumerate(reader.pages, 1):
    txt = (page.extract_text() or "").strip()
    if not txt:
        empty_pages += 1
    parts.append(f"\n## Page {i}\n\n{txt}\n")
text_md = "\n".join(parts)
print(f"Extracted: {len(text_md):,} chars  ({empty_pages} empty pages)")


# --- notes.md
notes_props = {
    "book": f"[[{BOOK_NAME}]]",
    "type": "reading-notes",
}
notes_md = f"""{yaml_block(notes_props)}

# Notes — {BOOK_NAME}

{nav()}

Append-only dated journal. Newest entries at the bottom.

Each entry: `## YYYY-MM-DD — ch. N §M` then prose. The compile pass folds
these into `wiki/concepts/*` and the book hub.

---

## {date.today().isoformat()} — initial ingest

{NOTES_PLACEHOLDER}
"""


# --- write
print()
print(f"PUT    /vault/{HUB_PATH:50s} -> {put_text(HUB_PATH, hub_md)}")
print(f"PUT    /vault/{TEXT_PATH:50s} -> {put_text(TEXT_PATH, text_md)}")
if has_user_content(NOTES_PATH, NOTES_PLACEHOLDER):
    print(f"SKIP   /vault/{NOTES_PATH:50s} (you have written notes)")
else:
    print(f"PUT    /vault/{NOTES_PATH:50s} -> {put_text(NOTES_PATH, notes_md)}")

# --- legacy cleanup (idempotent)
for legacy in LEGACY_PATHS:
    code = delete_path(legacy)
    print(f"DELETE /vault/{legacy:50s} -> {code}")

Confirmed:
- **Grimaldi textbook** (1005 pages): pure scan, 0 chars extractable — text.md will be a stub with a "needs OCR" warning
- **Solutions manual** (465 pages): has real text — full extraction works

I'll refactor `extract_book.py` into a multi-book processor that also handles the PDF upload (folding `ingest_pdf.py` in), with a `BOOKS` list at the top. It'll detect the scanned-PDF case and mark `extractable: false` in the hub frontmatter.

input.content:
"""One-shot — ingest one or more book PDFs into the vault.

For each book in BOOKS, writes:

    raw/<book>/
      <book>.md           <- HUB: YAML frontmatter + summary stub + nav
      source.pdf          <- the original PDF (binary PUT)
      text.md             <- extracted text (page-by-page) or "needs OCR" stub
      notes.md            <- your journal (only seeded if untouched)

Detects scanned/image-only PDFs (>95% of pages return empty text) and marks
`extractable: false` in the hub frontmatter, plus a warning callout in
text.md so the situation is visible in Obsidian.

Re-runnable. notes.md is overwritten only while the placeholder string is
still present.

Run: uv run python extract_book.py
"""

import json
import os
import sys
from datetime import date
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import quote

import requests
import urllib3
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from pypdf import PdfReader

load_dotenv(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / ".env")

API_KEY = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY")
HOST = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_HOST", "127.0.0.1")
PORT = int(os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_PORT", "27124"))
BASE = f"https://{HOST}:{PORT}"

if not API_KEY:
    sys.exit("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY not set in .env")

urllib3.disable_warnings(urllib3.exceptions.InsecureRequestWarning)

session = requests.Session()
session.headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {API_KEY}"
session.verify = False


BOOKS = [
    {
        "name": "Analisis Real - Lages",
        "pdf": Path(
            r"C:\Francisco\OneDrive - UNIVERSIDAD DE INGENIERIA Y TECNOLOGIA"
            r"\learn\books\Mathematics\PUCP\Fundamentos de analisis"
            r"\Analisis Real- Lages.pdf"
        ),
        "title": "Análisis Real",
        "author": "Elon Lages Lima",
        "language": "es",
        "course": "Fundamentos de Análisis",
        "institution": "PUCP",
        "tags": ["math", "real-analysis", "PUCP", "spanish"],
    },
    {
        "name": "Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e",
        "pdf": Path(
            r"C:\Francisco\OneDrive - UNIVERSIDAD DE INGENIERIA Y TECNOLOGIA"
            r"\learn\books\Mathematics\Discrete Mathematics"
            r"\Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e.pdf"
        ),
        "title": "Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics, 5th edition",
        "author": "Ralph P. Grimaldi",
        "language": "en",
        "course": None,
        "institution": None,
        "tags": ["math", "discrete-math", "combinatorics"],
    },
    {
        "name": "Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual)",
        "pdf": Path(
            r"C:\Francisco\OneDrive - UNIVERSIDAD DE INGENIERIA Y TECNOLOGIA"
            r"\learn\books\Mathematics\Discrete Mathematics"
            r"\Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics ,5e (Instructor's Solution Manual)(2004).pdf"
        ),
        "title": "Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics, 5e — Instructor's Solution Manual",
        "author": "Ralph P. Grimaldi",
        "language": "en",
        "course": None,
        "institution": None,
        "tags": ["math", "discrete-math", "combinatorics", "solutions-manual"],
    },
]

NOTES_PLACEHOLDER = "(write your first thoughts here)"


def put_text(path: str, body: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body.encode("utf-8"),
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=60,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def put_binary(path: str, body: bytes, mime: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body,
        headers={"Content-Type": mime},
        timeout=120,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def has_user_content(path: str, placeholder: str) -> bool:
    r = session.get(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        headers={"Accept": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=10,
    )
    return r.status_code == 200 and placeholder not in r.text


def yaml_str(v) -> str:
    if v is None:
        return "null"
    if isinstance(v, bool):
        return "true" if v else "false"
    if isinstance(v, (int, float)):
        return str(v)
    if isinstance(v, list):
        return "[" + ", ".join(yaml_str(x) for x in v) + "]"
    return json.dumps(str(v), ensure_ascii=False)


def yaml_block(d: dict) -> str:
    lines = ["---"]
    for k, v in d.items():
        lines.append(f"{k}: {yaml_str(v)}")
    lines.append("---")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def nav(folder: str, name: str, extra: str = "") -> str:
    lines = [
        f"> [!info]- {name} — navigation",
        f"> - **Book:** [[{name}]]",
        f"> - **Text:** [[{folder}/text|extracted text]]",
        f"> - **Notes:** [[{folder}/notes|notes]]",
        f"> - **PDF:** [[{folder}/source.pdf|source.pdf]]",
    ]
    if extra:
        lines.append(f"> - {extra}")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def process(book: dict) -> None:
    name = book["name"]
    pdf_path = book["pdf"]
    folder = f"raw/{name}"
    hub_path = f"{folder}/{name}.md"
    text_path = f"{folder}/text.md"
    notes_path = f"{folder}/notes.md"
    pdf_dest = f"{folder}/source.pdf"

    print(f"\n=== {name} ===")
    if not pdf_path.exists():
        print(f"  SKIP — PDF not found: {pdf_path}")
        return

    # 1. Upload PDF
    pdf_bytes = pdf_path.read_bytes()
    print(f"  PUT  {pdf_dest}  ({len(pdf_bytes)/1e6:.2f} MB)  -> {put_binary(pdf_dest, pdf_bytes, 'application/pdf')}")

    # 2. Extract text
    reader = PdfReader(pdf_path)
    n_pages = len(reader.pages)

    pages: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
    empty_pages = 0
    for i, page in enumerate(reader.pages, 1):
        txt = (page.extract_text() or "").strip()
        if not txt:
            empty_pages += 1
        pages.append((i, txt))
    extractable = empty_pages < n_pages * 0.95
    total_chars = sum(len(t) for _, t in pages)
    print(f"  pages: {n_pages}, empty: {empty_pages}, extractable: {extractable}, chars: {total_chars:,}")

    # 3. Build hub frontmatter
    m = reader.metadata or {}
    pdf_meta_title = str(m.get("/Title")) if m.get("/Title") else None
    pdf_meta_author = str(m.get("/Author")) if m.get("/Author") else None
    book_props = {
        "title": book.get("title") or pdf_meta_title or name,
        "author": book.get("author") or pdf_meta_author or "",
        "pages": n_pages,
        "language": book.get("language", "en"),
        "course": book.get("course"),
        "institution": book.get("institution"),
        "source_path": str(pdf_path),
        "ingested_at": date.today().isoformat(),
        "tags": book.get("tags", []),
        "status": "reading",
        "rating": None,
        "extractable": extractable,
    }

    # 4. Hub
    extraction_warning = ""
    if not extractable:
        extraction_warning = (
            "\n> [!warning] No text extracted\n"
            f"> The source PDF appears to be scanned/image-based — {empty_pages}/{n_pages} pages\n"
            "> returned no text. Run OCR on `source.pdf` (e.g. `ocrmypdf`) before the\n"
            "> compile pass can summarize this book.\n"
        )
    hub_md = f"""{yaml_block(book_props)}

# {name}

{nav(folder, name)}
{extraction_warning}
> [!warning]- Stub summary
> Placeholder. The compile pass replaces this with a chapter-by-chapter
> summary, key concepts, theorems, and exercises worth solving.
"""

    # 5. text.md
    text_props = {
        "book": f"[[{name}]]",
        "type": "extracted-text",
        "pages": n_pages,
        "extractable": extractable,
    }
    if not extractable:
        text_md = f"""{yaml_block(text_props)}

# {name} — extracted text

{nav(folder, name, f"**Pages:** {n_pages}")}

> [!warning] No text extracted
> The source PDF is scanned/image-based. pypdf returned 0 chars on
> {empty_pages} of {n_pages} pages. OCR the PDF first to populate this file.
"""
    else:
        parts = [
            yaml_block(text_props),
            "",
            f"# {name} — extracted text",
            "",
            nav(folder, name, f"**Pages:** {n_pages}"),
            "",
            f"Text extracted from `source.pdf` ({n_pages} pages).",
        ]
        for i, t in pages:
            parts.append(f"\n## Page {i}\n\n{t}\n")
        text_md = "\n".join(parts)

    # 6. notes.md
    notes_props = {
        "book": f"[[{name}]]",
        "type": "reading-notes",
    }
    notes_md = f"""{yaml_block(notes_props)}

# Notes — {name}

{nav(folder, name)}

Append-only dated journal. Newest entries at the bottom.

Each entry: `## YYYY-MM-DD — ch. N §M` then prose. The compile pass folds
these into `wiki/concepts/*` and the book hub.

---

## {date.today().isoformat()} — initial ingest

{NOTES_PLACEHOLDER}
"""

    print(f"  PUT  {hub_path}  -> {put_text(hub_path, hub_md)}")
    print(f"  PUT  {text_path}  -> {put_text(text_path, text_md)}")
    if has_user_content(notes_path, NOTES_PLACEHOLDER):
        print(f"  SKIP {notes_path}  (you have written notes)")
    else:
        print(f"  PUT  {notes_path}  -> {put_text(notes_path, notes_md)}")


for book in BOOKS:
    process(book)

print("\nDone.")

        return "[" + ", ".join(yaml_str(x) for x in v) + "]"
    return json.dumps(str(v), ensure_ascii=False)


def yaml_block(d: dict) -> str:
    lines = ["---"]
    for k, v in d.items():
        lines.append(f"{k}: {yaml_str(v)}")
    lines.append("---")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def nav(folder: str, name: str, extra: str = "") -> str:
    lines = [
        f"> [!info]- {name} — navigation",
        f"> - **Book:** [[{name}]]",
        f"> - **Text:** [[{folder}/text|extracted text]]",
        f"> - **Notes:** [[{folder}/notes|notes]]",
        f"> - **PDF:** [[{folder}/source.pdf|source.pdf]]",
    ]
    if extra:
        lines.append(f"> - {extra}")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def process(book: dict) -> None:
    name = book["name"]
    pdf_path = book["pdf"]
    folder = f"raw/{name}"
    hub_path = f"{folder}/{name}.md"
    text_path = f"{folder}/text.md"
    notes_path = f"{folder}/notes.md"
    pdf_dest = f"{folder}/source.pdf"

    print(f"\n=== {name} ===")
    if not pdf_path.exists():
        print(f"  SKIP — PDF not found: {pdf_path}")
        return

    # 1. Upload PDF
    pdf_bytes = pdf_path.read_bytes()
    print(f"  PUT  {pdf_dest}  ({len(pdf_bytes)/1e6:.2f} MB)  -> {put_binary(pdf_dest, pdf_bytes, 'application/pdf')}")

    # 2. Extract text
    reader = PdfReader(pdf_path)
    n_pages = len(reader.pages)

    pages: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
    empty_pages = 0
    for i, page in enumerate(reader.pages, 1):
        txt = (page.extract_text() or "").strip()
        if not txt:
            empty_pages += 1
        pages.append((i, txt))
    extractable = empty_pages < n_pages * 0.95
    total_chars = sum(len(t) for _, t in pages)
    print(f"  pages: {n_pages}, empty: {empty_pages}, extractable: {extractable}, chars: {total_chars:,}")

    # 3. Build hub frontmatter
    m = reader.metadata or {}
    pdf_meta_title = str(m.get("/Title")) if m.get("/Title") else None
    pdf_meta_author = str(m.get("/Author")) if m.get("/Author") else None
    book_props = {
        "title": book.get("title") or pdf_meta_title or name,
        "author": book.get("author") or pdf_meta_author or "",
        "pages": n_pages,
        "language": book.get("language", "en"),
        "course": book.get("course"),
        "institution": book.get("institution"),
        "source_path": str(pdf_path),
        "ingested_at": date.today().isoformat(),
        "tags": book.get("tags", []),
        "status": "reading",
        "rating": None,
        "extractable": extractable,
    }

    # 4. Hub
    extraction_warning = ""
    if not extractable:
        extraction_warning = (
            "\n> [!warning] No text extracted\n"
            f"> The source PDF appears to be scanned/image-based — {empty_pages}/{n_pages} pages\n"
            "> returned no text. Run OCR on `source.pdf` (e.g. `ocrmypdf`) before the\n"
            "> compile pass can summarize this book.\n"
        )
    hub_md = f"""{yaml_block(book_props)}

# {name}

{nav(folder, name)}
{extraction_warning}
> [!warning]- Stub summary
> Placeholder. The compile pass replaces this with a chapter-by-chapter
> summary, key concepts, theorems, and exercises worth solving.
"""

    # 5. text.md
    text_props = {
        "book": f"[[{name}]]",
        "type": "extracted-text",
        "pages": n_pages,
        "extractable": extractable,
    }
    if not extractable:
        text_md = f"""{yaml_block(text_props)}

# {name} — extracted text

{nav(folder, name, f"**Pages:** {n_pages}")}

> [!warning] No text extracted
> The source PDF is scanned/image-based. pypdf returned 0 chars on
> {empty_pages} of {n_pages} pages. OCR the PDF first to populate this file.
"""
    else:
        parts = [
            yaml_block(text_props),
            "",
            f"# {name} — extracted text",
            "",
            nav(folder, name, f"**Pages:** {n_pages}"),
            "",
            f"Text extracted from `source.pdf` ({n_pages} pages).",
        ]
        for i, t in pages:
            parts.append(f"\n## Page {i}\n\n{t}\n")
        text_md = "\n".join(parts)

    # 6. notes.md
    notes_props = {
        "book": f"[[{name}]]",
        "type": "reading-notes",
    }
    notes_md = f"""{yaml_block(notes_props)}

# Notes — {name}

{nav(folder, name)}

Append-only dated journal. Newest entries at the bottom.

Each entry: `## YYYY-MM-DD — ch. N §M` then prose. The compile pass folds
these into `wiki/concepts/*` and the book hub.

---

## {date.today().isoformat()} — initial ingest

{NOTES_PLACEHOLDER}
"""

    print(f"  PUT  {hub_path}  -> {put_text(hub_path, hub_md)}")
    print(f"  PUT  {text_path}  -> {put_text(text_path, text_md)}")
    if has_user_content(notes_path, NOTES_PLACEHOLDER):
        print(f"  SKIP {notes_path}  (you have written notes)")
    else:
        print(f"  PUT  {notes_path}  -> {put_text(notes_path, notes_md)}")


for book in BOOKS:
    process(book)

print("\nDone.")

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""One-shot — extract text + write the per-book layout to the vault.

Layout (book-folder-as-self-contained):

    raw/<book>/
      <book>.md           <- HUB: YAML frontmatter + summary stub + nav
      text.md             <- extracted text (regenerable, page-by-page)
      notes.md            <- your journal (only seeded if untouched)
      source.pdf          <- already uploaded by ingest_pdf.py

The hub lives next to the raw artifacts so each book is one self-contained
folder. wiki/ stays purely cross-cutting (concepts, authors, topics).

YAML frontmatter replaces the old metadata.json — Obsidian renders it as
Properties in the side panel and Dataview queries it directly. No orphan
JSON node in the graph.

Re-runnable. Cleans up legacy files (metadata.json, wiki/books/<book>.md)
on the first run. notes.md is overwritten only while the placeholder
string is still present.

Run: uv run python extract_book.py
"""

import json
import os
import sys
from datetime import date
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import quote

import requests
import urllib3
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from pypdf import PdfReader

load_dotenv(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / ".env")

API_KEY = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY")
HOST = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_HOST", "127.0.0.1")
PORT = int(os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_PORT", "27124"))
BASE = f"https://{HOST}:{PORT}"

if not API_KEY:
    sys.exit("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY not set in .env")

urllib3.disable_warnings(urllib3.exceptions.InsecureRequestWarning)

session = requests.Session()
session.headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {API_KEY}"
session.verify = False


LOCAL_PDF = Path(
    r"C:\Francisco\OneDrive - UNIVERSIDAD DE INGENIERIA Y TECNOLOGIA"
    r"\learn\books\Mathematics\PUCP\Fundamentos de analisis"
    r"\Analisis Real- Lages.pdf"
)
BOOK_NAME = "Analisis Real - Lages"
BOOK_FOLDER = f"raw/{BOOK_NAME}"
HUB_PATH = f"{BOOK_FOLDER}/{BOOK_NAME}.md"
TEXT_PATH = f"{BOOK_FOLDER}/text.md"
NOTES_PATH = f"{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes.md"

NOTES_PLACEHOLDER = "(write your first thoughts here)"

LEGACY_PATHS = [
    f"{BOOK_FOLDER}/metadata.json",
    f"wiki/books/{BOOK_NAME}.md",
    "Elon Lages Lima.md",
    "Real analysis.md",
]


def put_text(path: str, body: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body.encode("utf-8"),
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=60,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def delete_path(path: str) -> int:
    r = session.delete(f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}", timeout=10)
    return r.status_code


def has_user_content(path: str, placeholder: str) -> bool:
    """True if the file exists AND no longer contains the placeholder."""
    r = session.get(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        headers={"Accept": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=10,
    )
    return r.status_code == 200 and placeholder not in r.text


def yaml_str(v) -> str:
    """Quote a value for YAML — strings with special chars get double-quoted."""
    if v is None:
        return "null"
    if isinstance(v, bool):
        return "true" if v else "false"
    if isinstance(v, (int, float)):
        return str(v)
    if isinstance(v, list):
        return "[" + ", ".join(yaml_str(x) for x in v) + "]"
    s = str(v)
    return json.dumps(s, ensure_ascii=False)


def yaml_block(d: dict) -> str:
    lines = ["---"]
    for k, v in d.items():
        lines.append(f"{k}: {yaml_str(v)}")
    lines.append("---")
    return "\n".join(lines)


if not LOCAL_PDF.exists():
    sys.exit(f"PDF not found: {LOCAL_PDF}")

reader = PdfReader(LOCAL_PDF)
n_pages = len(reader.pages)
print(f"Source:    {LOCAL_PDF.name}")
print(f"Pages:     {n_pages}")

m = reader.metadata or {}
book_props = {
    "title": str(m.get("/Title") or "Análisis Real"),
    "author": str(m.get("/Author") or "Elon Lages Lima"),
    "pages": n_pages,
    "language": "es",
    "course": "Fundamentos de Análisis",
    "institution": "PUCP",
    "source_path": str(LOCAL_PDF),
    "ingested_at": date.today().isoformat(),
    "tags": ["math", "real-analysis", "PUCP", "spanish"],
    "status": "reading",
    "rating": None,
}


def nav(extra: str = "") -> str:
    lines = [
        f"> [!info]- {BOOK_NAME} — navigation",
        f"> - **Book:** [[{BOOK_NAME}]]",
        f"> - **Text:** [[{BOOK_FOLDER}/text|extracted text]]",
        f"> - **Notes:** [[{BOOK_FOLDER}/notes|notes]]",
        f"> - **PDF:** [[{BOOK_FOLDER}/source.pdf|source.pdf]]",
    ]
    if extra:
        lines.append(f"> - {extra}")
    return "\n".join(lines)


# --- hub: raw/<book>/<book>.md
hub_md = f"""{yaml_block(book_props)}

# {BOOK_NAME}

{nav()}

> [!warning]- Stub summary
> Placeholder. The compile pass replaces this with a chapter-by-chapter
> summary, key concepts, theorems, and exercises worth solving.
"""


# --- text.md
text_props = {
    "book": f"[[{BOOK_NAME}]]",
    "type": "extracted-text",
    "pages": n_pages,
}
parts = [
    yaml_block(text_props),
    "",
    f"# {BOOK_NAME} — extracted text",
    "",
    nav(f"**Pages:** {n_pages}"),
    "",
    f"Text extracted from `source.pdf` ({n_pages} pages).",
]
empty_pages = 0
for i, page in enumerate(reader.pages, 1):
    txt = (page.extract_text() or "").strip()
    if not txt:
        empty_pages += 1
    parts.append(f"\n## Page {i}\n\n{txt}\n")
text_md = "\n".join(parts)
print(f"Extracted: {len(text_md):,} chars  ({empty_pages} empty pages)")


# --- notes.md
notes_props = {
    "book": f"[[{BOOK_NAME}]]",
    "type": "reading-notes",
}
notes_md = f"""{yaml_block(notes_props)}

# Notes — {BOOK_NAME}

{nav()}

Append-only dated journal. Newest entries at the bottom.

Each entry: `## YYYY-MM-DD — ch. N §M` then prose. The compile pass folds
these into `wiki/concepts/*` and the book hub.

---

## {date.today().isoformat()} — initial ingest

{NOTES_PLACEHOLDER}
"""


# --- write
print()
print(f"PUT    /vault/{HUB_PATH:50s} -> {put_text(HUB_PATH, hub_md)}")
print(f"PUT    /vault/{TEXT_PATH:50s} -> {put_text(TEXT_PATH, text_md)}")
if has_user_content(NOTES_PATH, NOTES_PLACEHOLDER):
    print(f"SKIP   /vault/{NOTES_PATH:50s} (you have written notes)")
else:
    print(f"PUT    /vault/{NOTES_PATH:50s} -> {put_text(NOTES_PATH, notes_md)}")

# --- legacy cleanup (idempotent)
for legacy in LEGACY_PATHS:
    code = delete_path(legacy)
    print(f"DELETE /vault/{legacy:50s} -> {code}")

Got it. I'll merge into one Grimaldi folder with both PDFs as siblings, single text.md combining whatever's extractable per source, and clean up the now-orphan solutions folder.

Architecture for multi-PDF books:

```
raw/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e/
  Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e.md   <- hub (lists all sources in frontmatter)
  source.pdf                                                  <- main textbook (scanned)
  solutions.pdf                                               <- solutions manual
  text.md                                                     <- combined extraction, sectioned per source
  notes.md                                                    <- single journal
```

Refactoring `BOOKS` so each book has a `sources` list (role + filename + path).

"""One-shot — ingest one or more book PDFs into the vault.

Each book lives in `raw/<book>/` and may have multiple sources (textbook +
solutions manual + errata, etc.). All sources go into the same folder:

    raw/<book>/
      <book>.md           <- HUB: YAML frontmatter (with sources[]) + summary stub + nav
      source.pdf          <- primary PDF (role=textbook by convention)
      solutions.pdf       <- additional PDF (optional)
      text.md             <- combined extracted text, sectioned per source
      notes.md            <- single journal across all sources

Detects scanned/image-only PDFs (>95% empty pages) per source and marks
`extractable: false` on that source's entry in the hub frontmatter.

Re-runnable. notes.md is overwritten only while the placeholder is present.

Run: uv run python extract_book.py
"""

import json
import os
import sys
from datetime import date
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import quote

import requests
import urllib3
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from pypdf import PdfReader

load_dotenv(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / ".env")

API_KEY = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY")
HOST = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_HOST", "127.0.0.1")
PORT = int(os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_PORT", "27124"))

if not API_KEY:

urllib3.disable_warnings(urllib3.exceptions.InsecureRequestWarning)

session = requests.Session()
session.headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {API_KEY}"
session.verify = False

        "course": "Fundamentos de Análisis",
        "institution": "PUCP",
        "tags": ["math", "real-analysis", "PUCP", "spanish"],
        "sources": [
            {
                "role": "textbook",
                "filename": "source.pdf",
                "path": Path(
                    r"C:\Francisco\OneDrive - UNIVERSIDAD DE INGENIERIA Y TECNOLOGIA"
                    r"\learn\books\Mathematics\PUCP\Fundamentos de analisis"

            {
                "role": "textbook",

]

# Files left over from earlier ingest passes — deleted on each run (idempotent).
LEGACY_PATHS = [
    "raw/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual)/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual).md",
    "raw/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual)/source.pdf",
    "raw/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual)/text.md",
    "raw/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual)/notes.md",
]

NOTES_PLACEHOLDER = "(write your first thoughts here)"


def put_text(path: str, body: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body.encode("utf-8"),
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=60,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def put_binary(path: str, body: bytes, mime: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body,
        headers={"Content-Type": mime},
        timeout=180,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def delete_path(path: str) -> int:
    r = session.delete(f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}", timeout=10)
    return r.status_code


def has_user_content(path: str, placeholder: str) -> bool:
    r = session.get(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        headers={"Accept": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=10,
    )
    return r.status_code == 200 and placeholder not in r.text


def yaml_str(v) -> str:
    if v is None:
        return "null"
    if isinstance(v, bool):
        return "true" if v else "false"
    if isinstance(v, (int, float)):
        return str(v)
    if isinstance(v, list):
        return "[" + ", ".join(yaml_str(x) for x in v) + "]"
    return json.dumps(str(v), ensure_ascii=False)


def yaml_block(d: dict) -> str:
    """Top-level YAML block. Handles nested list-of-dicts for sources[]."""
    lines = ["---"]
    for k, v in d.items():
        if isinstance(v, list) and v and isinstance(v[0], dict):
            lines.append(f"{k}:")
            for item in v:
                first = True
                for ik, iv in item.items():
                    prefix = "  - " if first else "    "
                    lines.append(f"{prefix}{ik}: {yaml_str(iv)}")
                    first = False
        else:
            lines.append(f"{k}: {yaml_str(v)}")
    lines.append("---")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def nav(folder: str, name: str, sources: list[dict]) -> str:
    lines = [
        f"> [!info]- {name} — navigation",
        f"> - **Book:** [[{name}]]",
        f"> - **Text:** [[{folder}/text|extracted text]]",
        f"> - **Notes:** [[{folder}/notes|notes]]",
        "> - **Sources:**",
    ]
    for s in sources:
        label = s["role"]
        pages = s.get("pages")
        if pages is not None:
            label = f"{label} ({pages}p)"
        lines.append(f">   - [[{folder}/{s['filename']}|{label}]]")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def process(book: dict) -> None:
    name = book["name"]
    folder = f"raw/{name}"
    hub_path = f"{folder}/{name}.md"
    text_path = f"{folder}/text.md"
    notes_path = f"{folder}/notes.md"

    print(f"\n=== {name} ===")

    # 1. Upload PDFs + extract text per source
    source_blocks: list[dict] = []
    for src in book["sources"]:
        pdf_path: Path = src["path"]
        if not pdf_path.exists():
            print(f"  SKIP source {src['role']} — not found: {pdf_path}")
            continue
        pdf_bytes = pdf_path.read_bytes()
        dest = f"{folder}/{src['filename']}"
        print(f"  PUT  {dest}  ({len(pdf_bytes)/1e6:.2f} MB)  -> {put_binary(dest, pdf_bytes, 'application/pdf')}")

        reader = PdfReader(pdf_path)
        n_pages = len(reader.pages)
        pages: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
        empty = 0
        for i, page in enumerate(reader.pages, 1):
            txt = (page.extract_text() or "").strip()
            if not txt:
                empty += 1
            pages.append((i, txt))
        extractable = empty < n_pages * 0.95
        chars = sum(len(t) for _, t in pages)
        print(f"       pages: {n_pages}, empty: {empty}, extractable: {extractable}, chars: {chars:,}")

        source_blocks.append({
            "role": src["role"],
            "filename": src["filename"],
            "path": str(pdf_path),
            "pages": n_pages,
            "extractable": extractable,
            "_pages_text": pages,  # internal — stripped before frontmatter
            "_empty": empty,
        })

    if not source_blocks:
        print("  no sources found, skipping")
        return

    # 2. Hub frontmatter — strip private fields from sources
    sources_for_yaml = [
        {k: v for k, v in s.items() if not k.startswith("_")}
        for s in source_blocks
    ]
    book_props = {
        "title": book.get("title") or name,
        "author": book.get("author") or "",
        "language": book.get("language", "en"),
        "course": book.get("course"),
        "institution": book.get("institution"),
        "ingested_at": date.today().isoformat(),
        "tags": book.get("tags", []),
        "status": "reading",
        "rating": None,
        "sources": sources_for_yaml,
    }
    nav_block = nav(folder, name, sources_for_yaml)

    # 3. Hub
    fully_unextractable = all(not s["extractable"] for s in source_blocks)
    extraction_warning = ""
    if fully_unextractable:
        extraction_warning = (
            "\n> [!warning] No text extracted from any source\n"
            "> All source PDFs are scanned/image-based. OCR them before the\n"
            "> compile pass can summarize this book.\n"
        )

    hub_md = f"""{yaml_block(book_props)}

# {name}

{nav_block}
{extraction_warning}
> [!warning]- Stub summary
> Placeholder. The compile pass replaces this with a chapter-by-chapter
> summary, key concepts, theorems, and exercises worth solving.
"""

    # 4. text.md — section per source
    text_props = {
        "book": f"[[{name}]]",
        "type": "extracted-text",
    }
    text_parts = [
        yaml_block(text_props),
        "",
        f"# {name} — extracted text",
        "",
        nav_block,
        "",
    ]
    for s in source_blocks:
        header = f"## {s['filename']} — {s['role']} ({s['pages']} pages)"
        text_parts.append(header)
        if not s["extractable"]:
            text_parts.append(
                f"\n> [!warning] No text extracted\n"
                f"> Scanned PDF — pypdf returned no text on {s['_empty']} of {s['pages']} pages.\n"
                f"> OCR `{s['filename']}` to populate this section.\n"
            )
        else:
            for i, t in s["_pages_text"]:
                text_parts.append(f"\n### Page {i}\n\n{t}\n")
        text_parts.append("")
    text_md = "\n".join(text_parts)

    # 5. notes.md
    notes_props = {
        "book": f"[[{name}]]",
        "type": "reading-notes",
    }
    notes_md = f"""{yaml_block(notes_props)}

# Notes — {name}

{nav_block}

Append-only dated journal. Newest entries at the bottom.

Each entry: `## YYYY-MM-DD — ch. N §M` then prose. The compile pass folds
these into `wiki/concepts/*` and the book hub.

---

## {date.today().isoformat()} — initial ingest

{NOTES_PLACEHOLDER}
"""

    print(f"  PUT  {hub_path}  -> {put_text(hub_path, hub_md)}")
    print(f"  PUT  {text_path}  -> {put_text(text_path, text_md)}")
    if has_user_content(notes_path, NOTES_PLACEHOLDER):
        print(f"  SKIP {notes_path}  (you have written notes)")
    else:
        print(f"  PUT  {notes_path}  -> {put_text(notes_path, notes_md)}")


for book in BOOKS:
    process(book)

print("\n--- legacy cleanup ---")
for p in LEGACY_PATHS:
    print(f"DELETE /vault/{p}  -> {delete_path(p)}")

print("\nDone.")

"""One-shot — ingest one or more book PDFs into the vault.

    raw/<book>/
      <book>.md           <- HUB: YAML frontmatter (with sources[]) + summary stub + nav
      source.pdf          <- primary PDF (role=textbook by convention)
      solutions.pdf       <- additional PDF (optional)
      text.md             <- combined extracted text, sectioned per source
      notes.md            <- single journal across all sources

Detects scanned/image-only PDFs (>95% empty pages) per source and marks
`extractable: false` on that source's entry in the hub frontmatter.

Re-runnable. notes.md is overwritten only while the placeholder is present.

Run: uv run python extract_book.py

import json
import os

PORT = int(os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_PORT", "27124"))
BASE = f"https://{HOST}:{PORT}"

if not API_KEY:
    sys.exit("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY not set in .env")

urllib3.disable_warnings(urllib3.exceptions.InsecureRequestWarning)

session.headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {API_KEY}"
session.verify = False


BOOKS = [
    {
        "name": "Analisis Real - Lages",
        "title": "Análisis Real",
        "author": "Elon Lages Lima",

                "role": "solutions-manual",

                "path": Path(

                    r"\Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics ,5e (Instructor's Solution Manual)(2004).pdf"

LEGACY_PATHS = [
    "raw/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual)/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual).md",
    "raw/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual)/source.pdf",
    "raw/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual)/text.md",
    "raw/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual)/notes.md",
]

NOTES_PLACEHOLDER = "(write your first thoughts here)"


def put_text(path: str, body: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body.encode("utf-8"),
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=60,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def put_binary(path: str, body: bytes, mime: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body,
        headers={"Content-Type": mime},
        timeout=180,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def delete_path(path: str) -> int:
    r = session.delete(f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}", timeout=10)
    return r.status_code


def has_user_content(path: str, placeholder: str) -> bool:
    r = session.get(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        headers={"Accept": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=10,
    )
    return r.status_code == 200 and placeholder not in r.text


def yaml_str(v) -> str:
    if v is None:
        return "null"
    if isinstance(v, bool):
        return "true" if v else "false"
    if isinstance(v, (int, float)):

    if isinstance(v, list):

    return json.dumps(str(v), ensure_ascii=False)


def yaml_block(d: dict) -> str:

def process(book: dict) -> None:
    name = book["name"]

        reader = PdfReader(pdf_path)

    sources_for_yaml = [

        for s in source_blocks
    ]
    book_props = {
        "title": book.get("title") or name,
        "author": book.get("author") or "",
        "language": book.get("language", "en"),
        "course": book.get("course"),
        "institution": book.get("institution"),
        "ingested_at": date.today().isoformat(),

    nav_block = nav(folder, name, sources_for_yaml)

    # 3. Hub
    fully_unextractable = all(not s["extractable"] for s in source_blocks)
