toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""One-shot — ingest one or more book PDFs into the vault.

For each book in BOOKS, writes:

    raw/<book>/
      <book>.md           <- HUB: YAML frontmatter + summary stub + nav
      source.pdf          <- the original PDF (binary PUT)
      text.md             <- extracted text (page-by-page) or "needs OCR" stub
      notes.md            <- your journal (only seeded if untouched)

Detects scanned/image-only PDFs (>95% of pages return empty text) and marks
`extractable: false` in the hub frontmatter, plus a warning callout in
text.md so the situation is visible in Obsidian.

Re-runnable. notes.md is overwritten only while the placeholder string is
still present.

Run: uv run python extract_book.py
"""

import json
import os
import sys
from datetime import date
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import quote

import requests
import urllib3
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from pypdf import PdfReader

load_dotenv(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / ".env")

API_KEY = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY")
HOST = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_HOST", "127.0.0.1")
PORT = int(os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_PORT", "27124"))
BASE = f"https://{HOST}:{PORT}"

if not API_KEY:
    sys.exit("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY not set in .env")

urllib3.disable_warnings(urllib3.exceptions.InsecureRequestWarning)

session = requests.Session()
session.headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {API_KEY}"
session.verify = False


BOOKS = [
    {
        "name": "Analisis Real - Lages",
        "pdf": Path(
            r"C:\Francisco\OneDrive - UNIVERSIDAD DE INGENIERIA Y TECNOLOGIA"
            r"\learn\books\Mathematics\PUCP\Fundamentos de analisis"
            r"\Analisis Real- Lages.pdf"
        ),
        "title": "Análisis Real",
        "author": "Elon Lages Lima",
        "language": "es",
        "course": "Fundamentos de Análisis",
        "institution": "PUCP",
        "tags": ["math", "real-analysis", "PUCP", "spanish"],
    },
    {
        "name": "Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e",
        "pdf": Path(
            r"C:\Francisco\OneDrive - UNIVERSIDAD DE INGENIERIA Y TECNOLOGIA"
            r"\learn\books\Mathematics\Discrete Mathematics"
            r"\Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e.pdf"
        ),
        "title": "Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics, 5th edition",
        "author": "Ralph P. Grimaldi",
        "language": "en",
        "course": None,
        "institution": None,
        "tags": ["math", "discrete-math", "combinatorics"],
    },
    {
        "name": "Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual)",
        "pdf": Path(
            r"C:\Francisco\OneDrive - UNIVERSIDAD DE INGENIERIA Y TECNOLOGIA"
            r"\learn\books\Mathematics\Discrete Mathematics"
            r"\Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics ,5e (Instructor's Solution Manual)(2004).pdf"
        ),
        "title": "Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics, 5e — Instructor's Solution Manual",
        "author": "Ralph P. Grimaldi",
        "language": "en",
        "course": None,
        "institution": None,
        "tags": ["math", "discrete-math", "combinatorics", "solutions-manual"],
    },
]

NOTES_PLACEHOLDER = "(write your first thoughts here)"


def put_text(path: str, body: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body.encode("utf-8"),
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=60,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def put_binary(path: str, body: bytes, mime: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body,
        headers={"Content-Type": mime},
        timeout=120,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def has_user_content(path: str, placeholder: str) -> bool:
    r = session.get(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        headers={"Accept": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=10,
    )
    return r.status_code == 200 and placeholder not in r.text


def yaml_str(v) -> str:
    if v is None:
        return "null"
    if isinstance(v, bool):
        return "true" if v else "false"
    if isinstance(v, (int, float)):
        return str(v)
    if isinstance(v, list):
        return "[" + ", ".join(yaml_str(x) for x in v) + "]"
    return json.dumps(str(v), ensure_ascii=False)


def yaml_block(d: dict) -> str:
    lines = ["---"]
    for k, v in d.items():
        lines.append(f"{k}: {yaml_str(v)}")
    lines.append("---")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def nav(folder: str, name: str, extra: str = "") -> str:
    lines = [
        f"> [!info]- {name} — navigation",
        f"> - **Book:** [[{name}]]",
        f"> - **Text:** [[{folder}/text|extracted text]]",
        f"> - **Notes:** [[{folder}/notes|notes]]",
        f"> - **PDF:** [[{folder}/source.pdf|source.pdf]]",
    ]
    if extra:
        lines.append(f"> - {extra}")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def process(book: dict) -> None:
    name = book["name"]
    pdf_path = book["pdf"]
    folder = f"raw/{name}"
    hub_path = f"{folder}/{name}.md"
    text_path = f"{folder}/text.md"
    notes_path = f"{folder}/notes.md"
    pdf_dest = f"{folder}/source.pdf"

    print(f"\n=== {name} ===")
    if not pdf_path.exists():
        print(f"  SKIP — PDF not found: {pdf_path}")
        return

    # 1. Upload PDF
    pdf_bytes = pdf_path.read_bytes()
    print(f"  PUT  {pdf_dest}  ({len(pdf_bytes)/1e6:.2f} MB)  -> {put_binary(pdf_dest, pdf_bytes, 'application/pdf')}")

    # 2. Extract text
    reader = PdfReader(pdf_path)
    n_pages = len(reader.pages)

    pages: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
    empty_pages = 0
    for i, page in enumerate(reader.pages, 1):
        txt = (page.extract_text() or "").strip()
        if not txt:
            empty_pages += 1
        pages.append((i, txt))
    extractable = empty_pages < n_pages * 0.95
    total_chars = sum(len(t) for _, t in pages)
    print(f"  pages: {n_pages}, empty: {empty_pages}, extractable: {extractable}, chars: {total_chars:,}")

    # 3. Build hub frontmatter
    m = reader.metadata or {}
    pdf_meta_title = str(m.get("/Title")) if m.get("/Title") else None
    pdf_meta_author = str(m.get("/Author")) if m.get("/Author") else None
    book_props = {
        "title": book.get("title") or pdf_meta_title or name,
        "author": book.get("author") or pdf_meta_author or "",
        "pages": n_pages,
        "language": book.get("language", "en"),
        "course": book.get("course"),
        "institution": book.get("institution"),
        "source_path": str(pdf_path),
        "ingested_at": date.today().isoformat(),
        "tags": book.get("tags", []),
        "status": "reading",
        "rating": None,
        "extractable": extractable,
    }

    # 4. Hub
    extraction_warning = ""
    if not extractable:
        extraction_warning = (
            "\n> [!warning] No text extracted\n"
            f"> The source PDF appears to be scanned/image-based — {empty_pages}/{n_pages} pages\n"
            "> returned no text. Run OCR on `source.pdf` (e.g. `ocrmypdf`) before the\n"
            "> compile pass can summarize this book.\n"
        )
    hub_md = f"""{yaml_block(book_props)}

# {name}

{nav(folder, name)}
{extraction_warning}
> [!warning]- Stub summary
> Placeholder. The compile pass replaces this with a chapter-by-chapter
> summary, key concepts, theorems, and exercises worth solving.
"""

    # 5. text.md
    text_props = {
        "book": f"[[{name}]]",
        "type": "extracted-text",
        "pages": n_pages,
        "extractable": extractable,
    }
    if not extractable:
        text_md = f"""{yaml_block(text_props)}

# {name} — extracted text

{nav(folder, name, f"**Pages:** {n_pages}")}

> [!warning] No text extracted
> The source PDF is scanned/image-based. pypdf returned 0 chars on
> {empty_pages} of {n_pages} pages. OCR the PDF first to populate this file.
"""
    else:
        parts = [
            yaml_block(text_props),
            "",
            f"# {name} — extracted text",
            "",
            nav(folder, name, f"**Pages:** {n_pages}"),
            "",
            f"Text extracted from `source.pdf` ({n_pages} pages).",
        ]
        for i, t in pages:
            parts.append(f"\n## Page {i}\n\n{t}\n")
        text_md = "\n".join(parts)

    # 6. notes.md
    notes_props = {
        "book": f"[[{name}]]",
        "type": "reading-notes",
    }
    notes_md = f"""{yaml_block(notes_props)}

# Notes — {name}

{nav(folder, name)}

Append-only dated journal. Newest entries at the bottom.

Each entry: `## YYYY-MM-DD — ch. N §M` then prose. The compile pass folds
these into `wiki/concepts/*` and the book hub.

---

## {date.today().isoformat()} — initial ingest

{NOTES_PLACEHOLDER}
"""

    print(f"  PUT  {hub_path}  -> {put_text(hub_path, hub_md)}")
    print(f"  PUT  {text_path}  -> {put_text(text_path, text_md)}")
    if has_user_content(notes_path, NOTES_PLACEHOLDER):
        print(f"  SKIP {notes_path}  (you have written notes)")
    else:
        print(f"  PUT  {notes_path}  -> {put_text(notes_path, notes_md)}")


for book in BOOKS:
    process(book)

print("\nDone.")

import os, urllib3, requests

def walk(path=''):
    out = []
    r = s.get(f'{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}', timeout=5)
    if r.status_code != 200:
        return out
    for e in r.json().get('files', []):
        full = f'{path}{e}'
        if e.endswith('/'): out.extend(walk(full))
        else: out.append(full)
    return out

for p in walk(): print(p)

content.text:
Vault is clean — 2 books, 9 files total:

```
raw/Analisis Real - Lages/
  Analisis Real - Lages.md   (hub)
  source.pdf                  (1.45 MB, 236p, extractable)
  text.md                     (1 source)
  notes.md

raw/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e/
  Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e.md   (hub)
  source.pdf                  (41.5 MB, 1005p, scanned — needs OCR)
  solutions.pdf               (31.8 MB, 465p, extractable)
  text.md                     (2 sources, sectioned per PDF)
  notes.md
```

Open the Grimaldi hub in Obsidian — Properties panel will show the `sources` array with both PDFs (textbook with `extractable: false`, solutions with `extractable: true`), and the nav callout has links to both PDFs labeled by role + page count. The textbook section in `text.md` displays a "needs OCR" warning callout instead of empty page-by-page content.

Two quick observations on what we just learned:

1. **Multi-source books work cleanly** — one folder, one hub, one notes journal, multiple PDFs as siblings. Future books with errata or supplementary material slot in as another `sources[]` entry.
2. **Scanned PDFs need OCR before they're useful for the LLM**. The Grimaldi textbook is currently dead weight in the KB — Obsidian renders it (you can read it visually) but the LLM can't summarize it without OCR. If you want, the next step is to add an OCR pass (ocrmypdf) that runs before extraction when `extractable: false` is detected. That'd unlock the 1005-page Grimaldi for the eventual compile pass.

Want me to add OCR, or move on to scaffolding the actual MCP server?

content.text:
The ghost nodes (`151`, `!`, `2)`, `x-1`, etc.) are coming from `[[...]]` patterns inside the extracted PDF text. The Grimaldi solutions manual has lots of math notation — matrices, set notation, exercise references — that pypdf renders with double square brackets, and Obsidian dutifully parses every one as a wikilink, creating a ghost node per match.

Fix: escape `[[` → `\[\[` and `]]` → `\]\]` in the extracted text before writing. They'll still render as `[[...]]` in reading mode but won't trip the wikilink parser.

content.text:
Sound architectural call. Let me think through what we lose and gain before changing code.

**What text.md was for (Karpathy's pattern):** pre-extracted text the LLM reads during the wiki compile pass.

**Why dropping it is correct:**
1. **Lossy already** — pypdf-extracted math is degraded; you've seen the bracket spam, and there are likely other Obsidian-syntax collisions waiting (`#tag`, `^anchor`, `~~strike~~`, runaway code fences from indented LaTeX). Patching each is whack-a-mole.
2. **Vault bloat** — Lages alone is 392K chars, Grimaldi solutions 555K. They're files you'll never read directly (they're for the LLM), so they're pure noise in the file explorer and graph.
3. **Doesn't have to live in the vault.** When the compile pass runs, the LLM can either (a) read the PDF via pypdf on-the-fly, or (b) feed the PDF directly to Claude's multimodal API which handles PDFs natively — better than text extraction for math-heavy material since it sees the rendered formulas. Either way, no need to materialize a stale text.md.
4. **`extractable` becomes runtime, not static.** A book is "ready for compile" if at least one source PDF yields text or has been OCR'd. We can still flag it in frontmatter as a hint, but the cached text isn't part of the contract.

**What I'll change:**

- Drop `text.md` entirely (always delete any existing one — idempotent cleanup)
- Drop the `Text:` line from the nav callout
- Replace the slow per-page extraction with a 5-page sample probe just to set `extractable: true/false` in the frontmatter (cheap signal, no materialization)
- Remove `escape_obsidian()` — no longer needed once nothing extracted lands in the vault
- Keep the "needs OCR" warning on the hub when *all* sources are unextractable (real user-facing signal)
- The compile pass (later, in the MCP) reads PDFs at runtime via pypdf or Claude's PDF API

input.old_string:
"""One-shot — ingest one or more book PDFs into the vault.

Each book lives in `raw/<book>/` and may have multiple sources (textbook +
solutions manual + errata, etc.). All sources go into the same folder:

    raw/<book>/
      <book>.md           <- HUB: YAML frontmatter (with sources[]) + summary stub + nav
      source.pdf          <- primary PDF (role=textbook by convention)
      solutions.pdf       <- additional PDF (optional)
      text.md             <- combined extracted text, sectioned per source
      notes.md            <- single journal across all sources

Detects scanned/image-only PDFs (>95% empty pages) per source and marks
`extractable: false` on that source's entry in the hub frontmatter.

Re-runnable. notes.md is overwritten only while the placeholder is present.

Run: uv run python extract_book.py
"""
input.new_string:
"""One-shot — ingest one or more book PDFs into the vault.

Each book lives in `raw/<book>/` and may have multiple PDFs (textbook +
solutions + errata, etc.). All PDFs sit as siblings in the same folder:

    raw/<book>/
      <book>.md           <- HUB: YAML frontmatter (with sources[]) + summary stub + nav
      source.pdf          <- primary PDF (role=textbook by convention)
      solutions.pdf       <- additional PDF (optional)
      notes.md            <- single journal across all sources

No text.md. The PDF is the canonical source — the compile pass extracts
text at runtime (pypdf, or Claude's multimodal PDF API) when summarizing.
Materializing extracted text inflates the vault, breaks Obsidian syntax
(brackets, tags, anchors), and goes stale relative to the PDF.

A 5-page sample probe sets `extractable: true|false` in the hub frontmatter
so the user knows upfront whether the PDF needs OCR.

Re-runnable. notes.md is overwritten only while the placeholder is present.
Stale text.md from older runs is deleted.

Run: uv run python extract_book.py
"""

# 1. Upload PDFs + extract text per source
    source_blocks: list[dict] = []
    for src in book["sources"]:
        pdf_path: Path = src["path"]
        if not pdf_path.exists():
            print(f"  SKIP source {src['role']} — not found: {pdf_path}")
            continue
        pdf_bytes = pdf_path.read_bytes()
        dest = f"{folder}/{src['filename']}"
        print(f"  PUT  {dest}  ({len(pdf_bytes)/1e6:.2f} MB)  -> {put_binary(dest, pdf_bytes, 'application/pdf')}")

        reader = PdfReader(pdf_path)
        n_pages = len(reader.pages)
        pages: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
        empty = 0
        for i, page in enumerate(reader.pages, 1):
            txt = (page.extract_text() or "").strip()
            if not txt:
                empty += 1
            pages.append((i, escape_obsidian(txt)))
        extractable = empty < n_pages * 0.95
        chars = sum(len(t) for _, t in pages)
        print(f"       pages: {n_pages}, empty: {empty}, extractable: {extractable}, chars: {chars:,}")

        source_blocks.append({
            "role": src["role"],
            "filename": src["filename"],
            "path": str(pdf_path),
            "pages": n_pages,
            "extractable": extractable,
            "_pages_text": pages,  # internal — stripped before frontmatter
            "_empty": empty,

input.new_string:
# 1. Upload PDFs + sample-probe extractable
    source_blocks: list[dict] = []
    for src in book["sources"]:
        pdf_path: Path = src["path"]
        if not pdf_path.exists():
            print(f"  SKIP source {src['role']} — not found: {pdf_path}")
            continue
        pdf_bytes = pdf_path.read_bytes()
        dest = f"{folder}/{src['filename']}"
        print(f"  PUT  {dest}  ({len(pdf_bytes)/1e6:.2f} MB)  -> {put_binary(dest, pdf_bytes, 'application/pdf')}")

        reader = PdfReader(pdf_path)
        n_pages = len(reader.pages)
        sample = reader.pages[: min(5, n_pages)]
        sample_chars = sum(len((p.extract_text() or "").strip()) for p in sample)
        extractable = sample_chars > 100
        print(f"       pages: {n_pages}, extractable: {extractable} (sample: {sample_chars} chars)")

        source_blocks.append({
            "role": src["role"],
            "filename": src["filename"],
            "path": str(pdf_path),
            "pages": n_pages,
            "extractable": extractable,
        })

    if not source_blocks:
        print("  no sources found, skipping")
        return

    # 2. Hub frontmatter
    book_props = {
        "title": book.get("title") or name,
        "author": book.get("author") or "",
        "language": book.get("language", "en"),
        "course": book.get("course"),
        "institution": book.get("institution"),
        "ingested_at": date.today().isoformat(),
        "tags": book.get("tags", []),
        "status": "reading",
        "rating": None,
        "sources": source_blocks,
    }
    nav_block = nav(folder, name, source_blocks)

    extraction_warning = ""
    if all_unextractable:
        extraction_warning = (

1	"""One-shot — ingest one or more book PDFs into the vault.

14	Materializing extracted text inflates the vault, breaks Obsidian syntax

]

# Files left over from earlier ingest passes — deleted on each run (idempotent).
LEGACY_PATHS = [
    "raw/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual)/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual).md",
    "raw/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual)/source.pdf",
    "raw/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual)/text.md",
    "raw/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual)/notes.md",
]

NOTES_PLACEHOLDER = "(write your first thoughts here)"


def put_text(path: str, body: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body.encode("utf-8"),
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=60,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def put_binary(path: str, body: bytes, mime: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body,
        headers={"Content-Type": mime},
        timeout=180,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def delete_path(path: str) -> int:
    r = session.delete(f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}", timeout=10)
    return r.status_code


def has_user_content(path: str, placeholder: str) -> bool:
    r = session.get(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        headers={"Accept": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=10,
    )
    return r.status_code == 200 and placeholder not in r.text


def yaml_str(v) -> str:
    if v is None:
        return "null"
    if isinstance(v, bool):
        return "true" if v else "false"
    if isinstance(v, (int, float)):
        return str(v)
    if isinstance(v, list):
        return "[" + ", ".join(yaml_str(x) for x in v) + "]"
    return json.dumps(str(v), ensure_ascii=False)


def yaml_block(d: dict) -> str:
    """Top-level YAML block. Handles nested list-of-dicts for sources[]."""
    lines = ["---"]
    for k, v in d.items():
        if isinstance(v, list) and v and isinstance(v[0], dict):
            lines.append(f"{k}:")
            for item in v:
                first = True
                for ik, iv in item.items():
                    prefix = "  - " if first else "    "
                    lines.append(f"{prefix}{ik}: {yaml_str(iv)}")
                    first = False
        else:
            lines.append(f"{k}: {yaml_str(v)}")
    lines.append("---")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def nav(folder: str, name: str, sources: list[dict]) -> str:
    lines = [
        f"> [!info]- {name} — navigation",
        f"> - **Book:** [[{name}]]",
        f"> - **Notes:** [[{folder}/notes|notes]]",
        "> - **Sources:**",
    ]
    for s in sources:
        label = s["role"]
        pages = s.get("pages")
        if pages is not None:
            label = f"{label} ({pages}p)"
        lines.append(f">   - [[{folder}/{s['filename']}|{label}]]")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def process(book: dict) -> None:
    name = book["name"]
    folder = f"raw/{name}"
    hub_path = f"{folder}/{name}.md"
    text_path = f"{folder}/text.md"
    notes_path = f"{folder}/notes.md"

    print(f"\n=== {name} ===")

    # 1. Upload PDFs + sample-probe extractable
    source_blocks: list[dict] = []
    for src in book["sources"]:
        pdf_path: Path = src["path"]
        if not pdf_path.exists():
            print(f"  SKIP source {src['role']} — not found: {pdf_path}")
            continue
        pdf_bytes = pdf_path.read_bytes()
        dest = f"{folder}/{src['filename']}"
        print(f"  PUT  {dest}  ({len(pdf_bytes)/1e6:.2f} MB)  -> {put_binary(dest, pdf_bytes, 'application/pdf')}")

        reader = PdfReader(pdf_path)
        n_pages = len(reader.pages)
        sample = reader.pages[: min(5, n_pages)]
        sample_chars = sum(len((p.extract_text() or "").strip()) for p in sample)
        extractable = sample_chars > 100
        print(f"       pages: {n_pages}, extractable: {extractable} (sample: {sample_chars} chars)")

        source_blocks.append({
            "role": src["role"],
            "filename": src["filename"],
            "path": str(pdf_path),
            "pages": n_pages,
            "extractable": extractable,
        })

    if not source_blocks:
        print("  no sources found, skipping")
        return

    # 2. Hub frontmatter
    book_props = {
        "title": book.get("title") or name,
        "author": book.get("author") or "",
        "language": book.get("language", "en"),
        "course": book.get("course"),
        "institution": book.get("institution"),
        "ingested_at": date.today().isoformat(),
        "tags": book.get("tags", []),
        "status": "reading",
        "rating": None,
        "sources": source_blocks,
    }
    nav_block = nav(folder, name, source_blocks)

    # 3. Hub
    all_unextractable = all(not s["extractable"] for s in source_blocks)
    extraction_warning = ""
    if all_unextractable:
        extraction_warning = (
            "\n> [!warning] Needs OCR\n"
            "> No source PDF has extractable text. The compile pass can't\n"
            "> summarize this book until you OCR a source (e.g. `ocrmypdf`).\n"
        )

    hub_md = f"""{yaml_block(book_props)}

# {name}

{nav_block}
{extraction_warning}
> [!warning]- Stub summary
> Placeholder. The compile pass replaces this with a chapter-by-chapter
> summary, key concepts, theorems, and exercises worth solving.
"""

    # 5. notes.md
    notes_props = {
        "book": f"[[{name}]]",
        "type": "reading-notes",
    }
    notes_md = f"""{yaml_block(notes_props)}

# Notes — {name}

{nav_block}

Append-only dated journal. Newest entries at the bottom.

Each entry: `## YYYY-MM-DD — ch. N §M` then prose. The compile pass folds
these into `wiki/concepts/*` and the book hub.

---

## {date.today().isoformat()} — initial ingest

{NOTES_PLACEHOLDER}
"""

    print(f"  PUT  {hub_path}  -> {put_text(hub_path, hub_md)}")
    # Always drop any stale text.md from older runs.
    print(f"  DEL  {text_path}  -> {delete_path(text_path)}")
    if has_user_content(notes_path, NOTES_PLACEHOLDER):
        print(f"  SKIP {notes_path}  (you have written notes)")
    else:
        print(f"  PUT  {notes_path}  -> {put_text(notes_path, notes_md)}")


for book in BOOKS:
    process(book)

print("\n--- legacy cleanup ---")
for p in LEGACY_PATHS:
    print(f"DELETE /vault/{p}  -> {delete_path(p)}")

print("\nDone.")

input.new_string: # 4. notes.md

content.text:
Done. Final architecture per book:

```
raw/<book>/
  <book>.md     <- hub (frontmatter + nav + summary stub)
  *.pdf         <- one or more source PDFs
  notes.md      <- your single journal
```

Sample probe ran cleanly: Lages 2883 chars → `extractable: true`, Grimaldi textbook 0 chars → `extractable: false`, Grimaldi solutions 1847 chars → `extractable: true`. The Grimaldi hub doesn't show the "needs OCR" warning because at least one source is extractable; a fully scanned book would get the warning.

The graph is now minimal: per book, just hub ↔ notes ↔ PDF(s). No more bracket-ghost noise, no 392K-char text.md cluttering the file explorer, no concerns about extracted-text drift versus the actual PDF.

Reasonable next steps when you're ready:

1. **Scaffold the MCP server** (`server.py`, `obsidian_client.py`, `pyproject.toml`, `README.md`) — turns `ingest_book`, `read_pdf`, `append_notes`, `read_hub`, `write_hub` into FastMCP tools so this works from any Claude Code session, not just one-shot scripts.
2. **Compile pass design** — when invoked, the LLM reads PDF(s) directly via Claude's PDF API (no pypdf round-trip needed for math-heavy books since vision sees rendered formulas), reads the user's notes, and rewrites the hub's stub section with a real summary plus spawns `wiki/concepts/*.md` pages.

Both are doable in this session if you want to push further, but neither is urgent — the raw side is solid.

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user wants to build an LLM-driven Obsidian knowledge base for the math + CS books they're reading, following Karpathy's "LLM Knowledge Bases" pattern (raw/ for sources, wiki/ for LLM-compiled output). Specific intents that emerged through the session:

   - Upload book PDFs to the vault under `raw/<book>/source.pdf`

   - Drop unused/speculative scaffolding (READMEs, ghost author/topic links) when the user pointed them out
   - Treat the title file as the central node per book, living inside the book's own folder (`raw/<book>/<book>.md`) — no separate `wiki/books/`
   - Support multiple PDFs per book (textbook + solutions manual) in one folder
   - Final architectural decision (current state): drop `text.md` entirely from the vault — just PDFs, since extracted text is lossy, breaks Obsidian syntax (brackets, tags), inflates the vault, and can be re-extracted at runtime by the eventual compile pass

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Obsidian Local REST API plugin v3.6.1 by `coddingtonbear/obsidian-local-rest-api`

   - PUT auto-creates parent dirs (verified via Nia: `requestHandler.ts` calls `createFolder(path.dirname(filepath))` then `writeBinary` for non-string body)
   - DELETE /vault/{path} for file deletion (idempotent, 404 on missing)
   - GET /vault/{path}/ returns `{"files": [...]}` with `/`-suffixed dirs
   - Obsidian wikilinks `[[Name]]` resolve by basename; `\[\[` escapes them
   - Obsidian YAML frontmatter is queryable via Properties panel + Dataview
   - Obsidian `[!info]-` and `[!warning]-` collapsible callouts
   - Obsidian graph view: `.md` only by default; toggle "Attachments" for PDFs (which become edge-less nodes); ghost wikilinks render as dim nodes
   - pypdf's `PdfReader`, `.pages`, `.extract_text()`, `.outline`, `.metadata`
   - Karpathy's raw/wiki LLM Knowledge Base pattern
   - Multi-PDF books: `sources: [{role, filename, path, pages, extractable}]` in YAML frontmatter
   - Sample-probe pattern: extract first 5 pages, check char count > 100 to set `extractable: true/false` (cheaper than full extraction)

             ],

     def nav(folder, name, sources):
         lines = [

             "> - **Sources:**",
         ]
         for s in sources:
             label = s["role"]

             if pages is not None:

             lines.append(f">   - [[{folder}/{s['filename']}|{label}]]")
         return "\n".join(lines)

             sample = reader.pages[: min(5, n_pages)]
             sample_chars = sum(len((p.extract_text() or "").strip()) for p in sample)
             extractable = sample_chars > 100
             source_blocks.append({"role": ..., "filename": ..., "path": ..., "pages": n_pages, "extractable": extractable})
         # Hub frontmatter has sources[] list

file.content:
"""One-shot — ingest one or more book PDFs into the vault.

Each book lives in `raw/<book>/` and may have multiple PDFs (textbook +
solutions + errata, etc.). All PDFs sit as siblings in the same folder:

    raw/<book>/
      <book>.md           <- HUB: YAML frontmatter (with sources[]) + summary stub + nav
      source.pdf          <- primary PDF (role=textbook by convention)
      solutions.pdf       <- additional PDF (optional)
      notes.md            <- single journal across all sources

No text.md. The PDF is the canonical source — the compile pass extracts
text at runtime (pypdf, or Claude's multimodal PDF API) when summarizing.
Materializing extracted text inflates the vault, breaks Obsidian syntax
(brackets, tags, anchors), and goes stale relative to the PDF.

A 5-page sample probe sets `extractable: true|false` in the hub frontmatter
so the user knows upfront whether the PDF needs OCR.

Re-runnable. notes.md is overwritten only while the placeholder is present.
Stale text.md from older runs is deleted.

Run: uv run python extract_book.py
"""

import json
import os
import sys
from datetime import date
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import quote

import requests
import urllib3
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from pypdf import PdfReader

load_dotenv(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / ".env")

API_KEY = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY")
HOST = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_HOST", "127.0.0.1")
PORT = int(os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_PORT", "27124"))
BASE = f"https://{HOST}:{PORT}"

if not API_KEY:
    sys.exit("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY not set in .env")

urllib3.disable_warnings(urllib3.exceptions.InsecureRequestWarning)

session = requests.Session()
session.headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {API_KEY}"
session.verify = False


BOOKS = [
    {
        "name": "Analisis Real - Lages",
        "title": "Análisis Real",
        "author": "Elon Lages Lima",
        "language": "es",
        "course": "Fundamentos de Análisis",
        "institution": "PUCP",
        "tags": ["math", "real-analysis", "PUCP", "spanish"],
        "sources": [
            {
                "role": "textbook",
                "filename": "source.pdf",
                "path": Path(
                    r"C:\Francisco\OneDrive - UNIVERSIDAD DE INGENIERIA Y TECNOLOGIA"
                    r"\learn\books\Mathematics\PUCP\Fundamentos de analisis"
                    r"\Analisis Real- Lages.pdf"
                ),
            },
        ],
    },
    {
        "name": "Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e",
        "title": "Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics, 5th edition",
        "author": "Ralph P. Grimaldi",
        "language": "en",
        "course": None,
        "institution": None,
        "tags": ["math", "discrete-math", "combinatorics"],
        "sources": [
            {
                "role": "textbook",
                "filename": "source.pdf",
                "path": Path(
                    r"C:\Francisco\OneDrive - UNIVERSIDAD DE INGENIERIA Y TECNOLOGIA"
                    r"\learn\books\Mathematics\Discrete Mathematics"
                    r"\Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e.pdf"
                ),
            },
            {
                "role": "solutions-manual",
                "filename": "solutions.pdf",
                "path": Path(
                    r"C:\Francisco\OneDrive - UNIVERSIDAD DE INGENIERIA Y TECNOLOGIA"
                    r"\learn\books\Mathematics\Discrete Mathematics"
                    r"\Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics ,5e (Instructor's Solution Manual)(2004).pdf"
                ),
            },
        ],
    },
]

# Files left over from earlier ingest passes — deleted on each run (idempotent).
LEGACY_PATHS = [
    "raw/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual)/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual).md",
    "raw/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual)/source.pdf",
    "raw/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual)/text.md",
    "raw/Grimaldi - Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics 5e (Solutions Manual)/notes.md",
]

NOTES_PLACEHOLDER = "(write your first thoughts here)"


def put_text(path: str, body: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body.encode("utf-8"),
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=60,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def put_binary(path: str, body: bytes, mime: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body,
        headers={"Content-Type": mime},
        timeout=180,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


def delete_path(path: str) -> int:
    r = session.delete(f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}", timeout=10)
    return r.status_code


def has_user_content(path: str, placeholder: str) -> bool:
    r = session.get(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        headers={"Accept": "text/markdown"},
        timeout=10,
    )
    return r.status_code == 200 and placeholder not in r.text


def yaml_str(v) -> str:
    if v is None:
        return "null"
    if isinstance(v, bool):
        return "true" if v else "false"
    if isinstance(v, (int, float)):
        return str(v)
    if isinstance(v, list):
        return "[" + ", ".join(yaml_str(x) for x in v) + "]"
    return json.dumps(str(v), ensure_ascii=False)


def yaml_block(d: dict) -> str:
    """Top-level YAML block. Handles nested list-of-dicts for sources[]."""
    lines = ["---"]
    for k, v in d.items():
        if isinstance(v, list) and v and isinstance(v[0], dict):
            lines.append(f"{k}:")
            for item in v:
                first = True
                for ik, iv in item.items():
                    prefix = "  - " if first else "    "
                    lines.append(f"{prefix}{ik}: {yaml_str(iv)}")
                    first = False
        else:
            lines.append(f"{k}: {yaml_str(v)}")
    lines.append("---")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def nav(folder: str, name: str, sources: list[dict]) -> str:
    lines = [
        f"> [!info]- {name} — navigation",
        f"> - **Book:** [[{name}]]",
        f"> - **Notes:** [[{folder}/notes|notes]]",
        "> - **Sources:**",
    ]
    for s in sources:
        label = s["role"]
        pages = s.get("pages")
        if pages is not None:
            label = f"{label} ({pages}p)"
        lines.append(f">   - [[{folder}/{s['filename']}|{label}]]")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def process(book: dict) -> None:
    name = book["name"]
    folder = f"raw/{name}"
    hub_path = f"{folder}/{name}.md"
    text_path = f"{folder}/text.md"
    notes_path = f"{folder}/notes.md"

    print(f"\n=== {name} ===")

    # 1. Upload PDFs + sample-probe extractable
    source_blocks: list[dict] = []
    for src in book["sources"]:
        pdf_path: Path = src["path"]
        if not pdf_path.exists():
            print(f"  SKIP source {src['role']} — not found: {pdf_path}")
            continue
        pdf_bytes = pdf_path.read_bytes()
        dest = f"{folder}/{src['filename']}"
        print(f"  PUT  {dest}  ({len(pdf_bytes)/1e6:.2f} MB)  -> {put_binary(dest, pdf_bytes, 'application/pdf')}")

        reader = PdfReader(pdf_path)
        n_pages = len(reader.pages)
        sample = reader.pages[: min(5, n_pages)]
        sample_chars = sum(len((p.extract_text() or "").strip()) for p in sample)
        extractable = sample_chars > 100
        print(f"       pages: {n_pages}, extractable: {extractable} (sample: {sample_chars} chars)")

        source_blocks.append({
            "role": src["role"],
            "filename": src["filename"],
            "path": str(pdf_path),
            "pages": n_pages,
            "extractable": extractable,
        })

    if not source_blocks:
        print("  no sources found, skipping")
        return

    # 2. Hub frontmatter
    book_props = {
        "title": book.get("title") or name,
        "author": book.get("author") or "",
        "language": book.get("language", "en"),
        "course": book.get("course"),
        "institution": book.get("institution"),
        "ingested_at": date.today().isoformat(),
        "tags": book.get("tags", []),
        "status": "reading",
        "rating": None,
        "sources": source_blocks,
    }
    nav_block = nav(folder, name, source_blocks)

    # 3. Hub
    all_unextractable = all(not s["extractable"] for s in source_blocks)
    extraction_warning = ""
    if all_unextractable:
        extraction_warning = (
            "\n> [!warning] Needs OCR\n"
            "> No source PDF has extractable text. The compile pass can't\n"
            "> summarize this book until you OCR a source (e.g. `ocrmypdf`).\n"
        )

    hub_md = f"""{yaml_block(book_props)}

# {name}

{nav_block}
{extraction_warning}
> [!warning]- Stub summary
> Placeholder. The compile pass replaces this with a chapter-by-chapter
> summary, key concepts, theorems, and exercises worth solving.
"""

    # 4. notes.md
    notes_props = {
        "book": f"[[{name}]]",
        "type": "reading-notes",
    }
    notes_md = f"""{yaml_block(notes_props)}

# Notes — {name}

{nav_block}

Append-only dated journal. Newest entries at the bottom.

Each entry: `## YYYY-MM-DD — ch. N §M` then prose. The compile pass folds
these into `wiki/concepts/*` and the book hub.

---

## {date.today().isoformat()} — initial ingest

{NOTES_PLACEHOLDER}
"""

    print(f"  PUT  {hub_path}  -> {put_text(hub_path, hub_md)}")
    # Always drop any stale text.md from older runs.
    print(f"  DEL  {text_path}  -> {delete_path(text_path)}")
    if has_user_content(notes_path, NOTES_PLACEHOLDER):
        print(f"  SKIP {notes_path}  (you have written notes)")
    else:
        print(f"  PUT  {notes_path}  -> {put_text(notes_path, notes_md)}")


for book in BOOKS:
    process(book)

print("\n--- legacy cleanup ---")
for p in LEGACY_PATHS:
    print(f"DELETE /vault/{p}  -> {delete_path(p)}")

print("\nDone.")

"""One-shot — drop the 5 placeholder README pages and rewrite wiki/index.md.

The READMEs were scaffolding from the initial layout setup. They show up in
the graph view as a 5-node cluster with no real meaning. Deleting them and
pointing wiki/index.md at actual content makes the graph honest.

Re-running is safe — DELETE 404s and PUT is idempotent.

Run: uv run python cleanup_readmes.py
"""

import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import quote

import requests
import urllib3
from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / ".env")

API_KEY = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY")
HOST = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_HOST", "127.0.0.1")
PORT = int(os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_PORT", "27124"))
BASE = f"https://{HOST}:{PORT}"

if not API_KEY:
    sys.exit("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY not set in .env")

urllib3.disable_warnings(urllib3.exceptions.InsecureRequestWarning)

session = requests.Session()
session.headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {API_KEY}"
session.verify = False


TO_DELETE = [
    "raw/README.md",
    "wiki/books/README.md",
    "wiki/concepts/README.md",
    "wiki/authors/README.md",
    "wiki/topics/README.md",
]

INDEX = """# Knowledge base — index

Top-level map of content. Maintained by the LLM on compile passes.

## Books

- [[Analisis Real - Lages]]

## Concepts

_(empty — populated by compile pass from each book's text + notes)_

## Authors

- [[Elon Lages Lima]]

## Topics

- [[Real analysis]]
"""


for path in TO_DELETE:
    r = session.delete(f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}", timeout=10)
    print(f"DELETE /vault/{path:40s} -> {r.status_code}")

r = session.put(
    f"{BASE}/vault/{quote('wiki/index.md')}",
    data=INDEX.encode("utf-8"),
    headers={"Content-Type": "text/markdown"},
    timeout=10,
)
r.raise_for_status()
print(f"PUT    /vault/wiki/index.md{' ' * 28}-> {r.status_code}")

"""One-shot — copy a book PDF into the vault under raw/<folder>/source.pdf.

PUT auto-creates parent folders, so this also creates the book folder.
Re-running is safe — PUT is idempotent.

Run: uv run python ingest_pdf.py
"""

import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import quote

import requests
import urllib3
from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / ".env")

API_KEY = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY")
HOST = os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_HOST", "127.0.0.1")
PORT = int(os.getenv("OBSIDIAN_PORT", "27124"))
BASE = f"https://{HOST}:{PORT}"

if not API_KEY:
    sys.exit("OBSIDIAN_API_KEY not set in .env")

urllib3.disable_warnings(urllib3.exceptions.InsecureRequestWarning)

session = requests.Session()
session.headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {API_KEY}"
session.verify = False


SRC = Path(
    r"C:\Francisco\OneDrive - UNIVERSIDAD DE INGENIERIA Y TECNOLOGIA"
    r"\learn\books\Mathematics\PUCP\Fundamentos de analisis"
    r"\Analisis Real- Lages.pdf"
)
DEST = "raw/Analisis Real - Lages/source.pdf"


def put_binary(path: str, body: bytes, mime: str) -> int:
    r = session.put(
        f"{BASE}/vault/{quote(path)}",
        data=body,
        headers={"Content-Type": mime},
        timeout=60,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.status_code


if not SRC.exists():
    sys.exit(f"PDF not found: {SRC}")

pdf = SRC.read_bytes()
size_mb = len(pdf) / 1_000_000
print(f"Source: {SRC}")
print(f"Size:   {size_mb:.2f} MB")
print(f"Dest:   /vault/{DEST}")
print()

print(f"PUT -> {put_binary(DEST, pdf, 'application/pdf')}")

## damelo
Dámelo lets you share and manage AI assistant sessions (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) with your teams.

        Export and import all your sessions so nothing is missed. You can:
        - Export sessions with detailed summaries
        - List your own sessions
        - Browse sessions shared with your teams
        - Import sessions from teammates
        - Search sessions by repository

        All sessions are stored securely and can be shared with your team members.

        **CRITICAL: All session_data MUST be properly formatted HTML**

        The session_data parameter accepts ONLY valid HTML format. This will be saved as an .html file.

        **Required HTML formatting:**
        - Use proper HTML structure with <!DOCTYPE html>, <html>, <head>, and <body> tags
        - Use semantic HTML5 tags: <header>, <main>, <section>, <article>, <footer>
        - Use heading tags (<h1>, <h2>, <h3>, etc.) for document structure
        - Use <pre><code> blocks for code snippets, with class attribute for syntax highlighting (e.g., <code class="language-python">)
        - Use <ul>/<ol> and <li> for lists
        - Use <strong> and <em> for emphasis
        - Use <a href="url"> for links
        - Include proper CSS styling in <style> tags or inline for better presentation
        - Ensure all tags are properly closed and nested
        - At the end of the page always include a reference to damelo.sh
       
## drive
IMPORTANT: Always discover a tool's schema with ToolSearch BEFORE calling it for the first time.

Before executing any write operation (move, delete, create file, create folder), always tell the user exactly what you're about to do and STOP your turn. Do NOT call the tool in the same message. Wait for the user to reply with confirmation before making the call.

When presenting file results to the user, always:
- Show EVERY file — never truncate, summarize, or skip results
- Group by account (personal, work, university)
- Number each file sequentially within its account group (1., 2., 3.)
- Show the count per account
- Format: **file name** — type, size (e.g. **Invoice_2024.pdf** — PDF, 2.4 MB)
- Folders get a trailing / (e.g. **Documents/**)
- Show total count and total size at the end

When presenting file content (drive_read_file):
- Show the full file content — never truncate or summarize
- For CSV (Google Sheets), show the raw CSV data

When moving, deleting, or creating files (drive_update_files):
- action "move": moves files to a destination folder (server-side, no download)
- action "delete": trashes files (recoverable from Google Drive trash)
- action "create": creates new files with optional text content

You can pass either a Google Drive URL or a raw file/folder ID to any tool that accepts IDs.

Read-only tools (list, read, search) automatically try all configured accounts — no account parameter needed.
Write tools (update, create folder) require an explicit account parameter.
## glpi
IMPORTANT: Always discover a tool's schema with ToolSearch BEFORE calling it for the first time.

This server connects to a GLPI instance (IT service management) at ti.pulsosalud.com.

Ticket status codes and emojis (use the emoji when presenting status to the user):
  1=New       🆕
  2=Assigned  👤
  3=Planned   📅
  4=Waiting   ⏸️
  5=Solved    ✅
  6=Closed    🔒

When presenting ticket lists:
- Start each row with the time (HH:MM), then "[<emoji>] — <Status>", then " — ", then #id, title, category
- No numbered list; one ticket per line, in the order returned by the tool (tools default to chronological ascending — oldest first)
- Use expand_dropdowns=true when showing data to users (names instead of IDs)
- For search results, field IDs map to: 1=name, 2=id, 7=category, 12=status, 15=date_creation

Example row:
  06:56 [🆕] — Status — #32654 ELIMINAR ATENCION · ANA Prevention
## gmail
IMPORTANT: Always discover a tool's schema with ToolSearch BEFORE calling it for the first time.

Before executing any write operation (trash, tag, send, create draft), always tell the user exactly what you're about to do and STOP your turn. Do NOT call the tool in the same message. Wait for the user to reply with confirmation before making the call.

When presenting email results to the user, always:
- Group by account (personal, work, university)
- Number each email sequentially within its account group (1., 2., 3.)
- Show the count per account
- Bold the sender name: **Sender** — Subject

Tag system for organizing emails:
- "important" → starred emails — actionable, urgent, needs attention
- "credentials" → passwords, API keys, server access, login details
- "contacts" → emails from people the user cares about maintaining a relationship with
Custom tags beyond these three are also supported and auto-created on first use.

Auto-sorted emails:
- Emails tagged with "ai/*" labels (e.g. "ai/finance") have already been reviewed and sorted by the AI.
- gmail_search_messages always excludes ai/* emails and returns their counts in "ai_skipped".
- Always show the ai_skipped summary to the user (e.g. "Also sorted: ai/programming (1), ai/finance (3)").
- To see auto-sorted emails, use gmail_get_tagged("ai/finance") — NOT gmail_search_messages.

Search behavior:
- When the user asks to "show emails", "check email", or similar — just call gmail_search_messages immediately. Do not plan, ask clarifying questions, or add extra steps.

Attachments:
- When gmail_read_message downloads attachments, immediately read them using the Read tool — do NOT ask the user first. The "hint" field in each attachment tells you the file path to read.
- Reading is not a write operation — no confirmation needed.
- For binary files (Excel .xlsx, .pptx, etc.), the Read tool won't work. Use Python (openpyxl, pandas) or the appropriate skill to read them instead.
## jira
This server reads from a Jira Cloud instance (configured via JIRA_URL in .env).

All tools are READ-ONLY. No writes, comments, transitions, or edits are exposed.

When the user asks about "tickets", "issues", or "tasks" — use jira_search_issues with JQL.
Jira Cloud requires a bounded JQL query — always include at least one constraint
(project = X, created >= -30d, assignee = currentUser(), status = "Open", etc.)
before any ORDER BY.

All listing tools (jira_search_issues, jira_search_users, jira_list_boards,
jira_list_board_sprints, jira_get_sprint_issues) auto-paginate and return the
full result set as {count, <key>}. There are no limit / start / nextPageToken
flags — scope the query itself if you need to narrow results.

jira_get_issue returns the full ticket in one call: base fields + changelog +
status history + comments + worklog + watchers + attachments + available
transitions. Don't fan out into multiple sub-calls — everything is inlined.

jira_get_project returns project metadata + components + versions + issue count
in one call.

jira_list_metadata returns all fields (system + custom) + statuses + priorities
in one call — use it to resolve custom field IDs or status names before JQL.

When presenting issues, format as:
  [STATUS] KEY-123 Summary (assignee · YYYY-MM-DD)
## music
IMPORTANT: Always discover a tool's schema with ToolSearch BEFORE calling it for the first time.

When the user asks to play music — whether "play a song", "play something", "play a calming song", "play something upbeat", etc. — ALWAYS use the user's Liked Songs as the source. Never search Spotify. The flow is:
1. Call spotify_list_tracks("liked") to get their Liked Songs
2. Pick a song that matches what they asked for (e.g. calming, energetic, etc.)
3. Play it with spotify_play

If they say "play something" with no preference, use spotify_play with random=true for a random pick.

Do NOT ask for confirmation before playing. Just play the song immediately and tell the user what's playing.

When presenting track results, format as:
  1. **Track Name** — Artist(s) · Album
     URI: spotify:track:xxx

When presenting playlists, format as:
  1. **Playlist Name** — N tracks · owner
## whatsapp
IMPORTANT: Always discover a tool's schema with ToolSearch BEFORE calling it for the first time.

To read WhatsApp messages:
1. Call whatsapp_list_chats to find the chat and grab its id.
   - Requires at least one of: query (name/phone substring), tag, or since (ISO timestamp).
2. Pass that id into whatsapp_get_messages to read the conversation.

CRITICAL — whatsapp_get_messages returns pre-formatted conversation output.
You MUST paste the ENTIRE text content into your response as a verbatim code block.
Do NOT summarize, paraphrase, abbreviate, or skip any messages. Show EVERY line.

When whatsapp_download_files returns file content, save each file to ~/Downloads
with its original filename (the token name, e.g. "hash.jpg").

content.text:
Based on the architecture, here's what I'd ship as v1.

## Tool surface (8 typed + 3 escape-hatch)

**Reading + journal (the main loop, called every session):**

| Tool | Signature | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `obsidian_list_books` | `()` | Scan `raw/*/` for hubs, return `[{name, title, author, status, pages_total, n_sources, extractable_any}]` — what's in the vault, one row per book |
| `obsidian_read_hub` | `(name)` | Return hub `.md` + parsed frontmatter + extracted summary section. Use when user asks "what's in book X" |
| `obsidian_read_notes` | `(name, since?: ISO date)` | Return `notes.md` body, optionally filtered to entries ≥ date |
| `obsidian_append_notes` | `(name, chapter?, section?, text)` | Append `## YYYY-MM-DD — ch.N §M\n\n{text}\n` to `notes.md`. No confirm — journal appends are cheap and accretive |
| `obsidian_read_pdf` | `(name, source?: filename, pages?: "1-20" \| [1,2,5])` | pypdf-extract text. Default source = role=textbook. If extractable=false, returns error pointing to OCR — don't try |

**Ingest + lifecycle (occasional):**

| Tool | Signature | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `obsidian_ingest_book` | `(name, title, author, sources: [{role, local_path, filename?}], language?, course?, institution?, tags?)` | Folds in `extract_book.py`. Idempotent — if book exists and source has same filename, re-uploads + re-probes. Adds new source if filename is new |
| `obsidian_update_hub` | `(name, status?, rating?, tags?: list, summary_md?)` | Patches frontmatter + replaces stub summary section. Body of nav callout untouched |
| `obsidian_remove_book` | `(name)` | Deletes `raw/<name>/` recursively. Confirm before |

**Generic escape hatch (rare, for wiki/* until compile design firms up):**

| Tool | Signature |
|---|---|
| `obsidian_list_path(path?)` |
| `obsidian_read_file(path)` |
| `obsidian_write_file(path, content)` — confirm before |

Deferred until the compile workflow gels: typed `write_concept` / `update_index` / wiki helpers.

## MCP instructions block

```
This server reads/writes a personal Obsidian KB of math + CS books at:
  raw/<book>/<book>.md     (hub: YAML frontmatter + nav + summary)
  raw/<book>/*.pdf         (one or more sources: textbook, solutions, errata)
  raw/<book>/notes.md      (single dated journal across all sources)
  wiki/                    (LLM-compiled output — DO NOT write ad-hoc)

IMPORTANT: discover schemas with ToolSearch before first call.

ACTIVE READING LOOP — when the user shares a thought while reading
("just read ch.3 §2.4 on Cauchy sequences, this is interesting because…"),
call obsidian_append_notes IMMEDIATELY. Do not paraphrase — log their
prose. Always pass chapter/section if the user names them. No
confirmation needed for journal appends.

PDF READS — obsidian_read_pdf for a page range, never the whole book by
default (chapter-sized: 20–40 pages). If a source has extractable=false,
DO NOT call read_pdf — tell the user the PDF is scanned and needs
ocrmypdf. Check obsidian_read_hub or obsidian_list_books to see the
extractable flag before reading.

WRITE CONFIRMATION:
  - obsidian_ingest_book   → confirm first (uploads MB-scale binary)
  - obsidian_remove_book   → confirm first (irreversible)
  - obsidian_write_file    → confirm first (escape hatch, can clobber)
  - obsidian_update_hub    → just do it (frontmatter patch)
  - obsidian_append_notes  → just do it (append-only)

WIKI/ IS COMPILED OUTPUT — never write to wiki/* unless the user
explicitly asks for a "compile pass" or "rebuild concepts". The raw/
side is the source of truth; wiki/ is regenerated from it.

NEW FOLDER RULE — do not create top-level folders. Only raw/<book>/,
notes/, wiki/, attachments/ exist by design.

PRESENTATION:
  Book list:  **Title** — Author · status · N pages across M sources
              (mark "needs OCR" if no source is extractable)
  Hub display: title · author · status · sources[] (role + page count +
              extractable flag) · current summary section
  Notes display: chronological as written (don't reverse).
```

That keeps the AI from drifting into wiki/, prevents pointless OCR-failure round trips, and makes the journal-append loop frictionless — which is the workflow you actually live in. Want me to scaffold `server.py` + `obsidian_client.py` with these tools next?

content.text:
## The principle

Three tools, two concerns: **eyes** (graph) and **hands** (read/write). The AI sees the whole knowledge graph in one call, then does precision IO on individual `.md` nodes. PDFs appear in the graph but aren't readable through the tool — they're for the human to read, the AI works through the markdown layer. That's the cleanest possible LLM-KB loop.

## Tool signatures

```python
get_raw_base() -> dict
    # Whole-vault graph in one call. No params, cheap, idempotent.
    # AI calls this at session start to orient.

read(path: str) -> str
    # path must end in .md (else InputValidationError).
    # Returns full markdown body (frontmatter included).

write(path: str, content: str) -> dict
    # path must end in .md (else InputValidationError).
    # Full overwrite — creates or replaces. No append mode.
    # To "append," the AI does read → modify in memory → write.
    # Returns {"path": str, "bytes": int, "created": bool}.
```

## Graph format

The shape that maps cleanly to Obsidian's actual model:

```json
{
  "nodes": [
    {
      "path": "raw/Analisis Real - Lages/Analisis Real - Lages.md",
      "type": "hub",
      "frontmatter": {
        "title": "Análisis Real",
        "author": "Elon Lages Lima",
        "status": "reading",
        "tags": ["math","real-analysis","PUCP","spanish"],
        "sources": [{"role":"textbook","filename":"source.pdf","pages":236,"extractable":true}]
      }
    },
    { "path": "raw/Analisis Real - Lages/notes.md", "type": "notes",
      "frontmatter": {"book":"[[Analisis Real - Lages]]","type":"reading-notes"} },
    { "path": "raw/Analisis Real - Lages/source.pdf", "type": "pdf",
      "pages": 236, "extractable": true, "size_bytes": 1450123 }
  ],
  "edges": [
    { "from":"raw/Analisis Real - Lages/Analisis Real - Lages.md",
      "to":"raw/Analisis Real - Lages/notes.md",
      "kind":"wikilink", "target_exists": true },
    { "from":"raw/Analisis Real - Lages/Analisis Real - Lages.md",
      "to":"raw/Analisis Real - Lages/source.pdf",
      "kind":"wikilink", "target_exists": true },
    { "from":"raw/Analisis Real - Lages/notes.md",
      "to":"Analisis Real - Lages",
      "kind":"frontmatter", "target_exists": true }
  ]
}
```

Five node `type`s the AI can branch on: `hub`, `notes`, `concept`, `index`, `pdf`, `other_md`. Edge `kind`s: `wikilink`, `embed`, `frontmatter`. `target_exists: false` flags ghost links — gaps the AI can offer to fill.

The graph **must** include:
- All `.md` and `.pdf` nodes (PDFs as edge-less leaves with extractability flag)
- Wikilinks `[[X]]` and `[[X|alias]]` and `[[X#anchor]]` resolved by basename
- Embeds `![[X]]` (same resolution, different `kind`)
- Frontmatter wikilinks (notes.md's `book: [[X]]` is a real edge)

Keep node entries metadata-only — no body content. The AI uses `read()` to drill in.

## MCP instructions block (the rules)

```
Personal Obsidian KB of math + CS books. Layout:
  raw/<book>/<book>.md   (hub: frontmatter + nav callout + summary)
  raw/<book>/*.pdf       (sources: textbook, solutions, errata, ...)
  raw/<book>/notes.md    (single dated journal across all sources)
  wiki/                  (LLM-compiled output — you write here only on
                          explicit "compile" / "rebuild concepts" requests)

THREE TOOLS — discover schemas with ToolSearch first.

ALWAYS START WITH get_raw_base. Don't read blindly. The graph tells you
what exists, the node types, the wikilinks, and which links are ghosts
(target_exists: false). All read/write decisions follow from it.

PDFs ARE NOT READABLE. They appear in the graph as nodes (with page
count + extractable flag) so you know they exist, but `read` is .md-only.
If the user asks "what does ch.3 of Lages say?", you cannot answer from
the PDF directly. Offer to: (a) read their notes.md for what they wrote
about ch.3, (b) summarize what the hub already records, or (c) ask them
to paste the passage into notes.md. Do not pretend to have read the PDF.

APPEND = READ-MODIFY-WRITE. `write` is a full overwrite. To add a journal
entry to notes.md, you MUST read it first, append the new entry to the
existing body, then write the whole thing back. Never write partial
content — you'll silently destroy prior entries.

PRESERVE FRONTMATTER. When editing any .md, never drop, reorder, or
silently mutate the YAML frontmatter unless the user explicitly asked
for it. Frontmatter holds the queryable schema (status, sources,
ingested_at, tags) — it's not decoration. Same for the nav callout in
hub pages — it's the connectivity backbone for the graph.

WIKILINK HYGIENE. When you write content that references a book or
concept, use [[Exact Basename]] from get_raw_base. Don't invent ghost
links unless intentional. If you're forced to reference something that
doesn't exist yet, mention it explicitly: "this links to a concept page
that doesn't exist — want me to create it?"

NO NEW TOP-LEVEL FOLDERS. Only raw/, wiki/, attachments/ exist by design.
New books go in raw/<book>/. New concepts go in wiki/concepts/. Don't
invent siblings of these.

JOURNAL APPEND FORMAT. notes.md uses dated headings:
    ## YYYY-MM-DD — ch.N §M
    {user's prose, verbatim — don't paraphrase}
Always include chapter/section if the user names them. Use today's date
in the user's local time.

CONFIRM BEFORE WRITE. Writes are persistent and overwriting. Tell the
user the path and a short summary of what's changing, then stop your
turn and wait for confirmation. Exception: if the user pre-authorized
("just log this", "append this thought"), proceed without re-asking.

PRESENTATION when showing the graph to the user:
- Group by folder (one section per book in raw/)
- For each book: **Title** — Author · status · sources (mark "needs OCR"
  if all sources are unextractable)
- Surface ghost links explicitly — they're gaps in the KB
- Don't dump the raw JSON; render it as prose + nested lists.
```

## What this trades off

You're accepting that **the AI can't see inside PDFs in v1**. For the active-reading loop (you read → you journal → AI helps think) that's fine — markdown is the human↔AI channel. But the eventual compile pass ("AI, summarize Lages chapter 3 into a wiki concept") will need PDF reads. When that comes, add a fourth tool — `read_pdf(path, pages?)` returning extracted text — without disturbing the existing three. The graph already carries `extractable: true|false`, so the AI will know whether `read_pdf` would even work before it tries.

One naming nit: `get_raw_base` reads like "the raw/ folder index" but the spec above includes the whole vault. Either rename to `get_graph` / `get_index`, or genuinely scope it to `raw/` and add a sibling later for `wiki/`. I'd vote `get_graph` — it's what the tool actually returns and matches Obsidian's terminology.

content.content:
1	# MCP Servers
2	
3	How to build and configure MCP servers in this repo.
4	
5	## What is an MCP server?
6	
7	An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server exposes tools, resources, and prompts to AI clients like Claude Code, Claude Desktop, and Cursor. Each server lives in `mcps/` and provides a focused set of capabilities (Gmail, WhatsApp, Drive, etc.).
8	
9	## Structure
10	
11	```
12	mcps/
13	  my-server/
14	    server.py / mcp-server.js   # Required — the MCP server entry point
15	    README.md                   # Required — tools table, setup, usage
16	    requirements.txt / package.json
17	    icon_48x48.png              # Optional — icon for Claude Desktop
18	```
19	
20	## Adding an icon for Claude Desktop
21	
22	MCP servers can declare an icon that shows up in Claude Desktop's Connectors menu. The icon is served during the MCP initialization handshake via the `icons` field in `serverInfo`.
23	
24	### Steps
25	
26	#### 1. Create a 48x48 PNG
27	
28	Resize your icon to 48x48 pixels and save it alongside your server code:
29	
30	```python
31	from PIL import Image
32	img = Image.open("icon.png")
33	img = img.resize((48, 48), Image.LANCZOS)
34	img.save("icon_48x48.png")
35	```
36	
37	#### 2. Add it to serverInfo
38	
39	**TypeScript (MCP SDK):**
40	
41	```js
42	import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
43	import { join } from "node:path";
44	
45	// For Electron apps, use app.getAppPath() instead of __dirname
46	const ICON_B64 = readFileSync(join(app.getAppPath(), "icon_48x48.png")).toString("base64");
47	
48	const server = new McpServer({
49	  name: "my-server",
50	  version: "1.0.0",
51	  icons: [{
52	    src: `data:image/png;base64,${ICON_B64}`,
53	    mimeType: "image/png",
54	    sizes: ["48x48"],
55	  }],
56	});
57	```
58	
59	**Python (FastMCP):**
60	
61	```python
62	import base64
63	from pathlib import Path
64	
65	icon_b64 = base64.b64encode(Path("icon_48x48.png").read_bytes()).decode()
66	
67	# Pass icons in the server metadata (check FastMCP docs for exact API)
68	```
69	
70	#### 3. For Electron apps: include in the build
71	
72	Add the icon file to the `files` array in `package.json` so electron-builder bundles it:
73	
74	```json
75	{
76	  "build": {
77	    "files": [
78	      "mcp-server.js",
79	      "icon_48x48.png"
80	    ]
81	  }
82	}
83	```
84	
85	Use `app.getAppPath()` (not `__dirname`) to resolve the file path — `__dirname` points to a different location in packaged Electron apps.
86	
87	### Icon spec (MCP 2025-11-25)
88	
89	| Field | Required | Description |
90	|-------|----------|-------------|
91	| `src` | Yes | Data URI (`data:image/png;base64,...`) or HTTPS URL |
92	| `mimeType` | No | `image/png`, `image/jpeg`, `image/svg+xml`, `image/webp` |
93	| `sizes` | No | Array of size strings, e.g. `["48x48"]` |
94	| `theme` | No | `light` or `dark` |
95	
96	Claude Desktop must support PNG and JPEG. SVG and WebP are optional.
97	
98	## Connecting to Claude Desktop
99	
100	Claude Desktop only supports **stdio** for local MCP servers. If your server runs over HTTP (like an Electron app), use a bridge:
101	
102	### Direct stdio server
103	
104	```json
105	{
106	  "mcpServers": {
107	    "my-server": {
108	      "command": "uv",
109	      "args": ["run", "--directory", "/path/to/mcps/my-server", "fastmcp", "run", "server.py"]
110	    }
111	  }
112	}
113	```
114	
115	### HTTP server via supergateway bridge
116	
117	For servers that expose an HTTP endpoint (e.g. `http://localhost:PORT/mcp`), use [supergateway](https://github.com/supercorp-ai/supergateway) to bridge HTTP to stdio:
118	
119	```json
120	{
121	  "mcpServers": {
122	    "my-server": {
123	      "command": "cmd",
124	      "args": ["/c", "npx", "-y", "supergateway", "--streamableHttp", "http://localhost:PORT/mcp"]
125	    }
126	  }
127	}
128	```
129	
130	On macOS/Linux, drop the `"cmd", "/c"` prefix.
131	
132	Config file location:
133	- **Windows:** `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json`
134	- **macOS:** `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`
135	
136	## Credentials
137	
138	Store secrets in the repo root `.env` (already gitignored). Servers load them with `python-dotenv` and pass explicitly via `os.getenv()`:
139	
140	```python
141	import os
142	from dotenv import load_dotenv
143	from pathlib import Path
144	
145	load_dotenv(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / ".env")
146	
147	# Always pass credentials explicitly — don't rely on libraries reading env vars implicitly
148	client = SomeAPI(
149	    api_key=os.getenv("MY_API_KEY"),
150	    secret=os.getenv("MY_API_SECRET"),
151	)
152	```
153	
154	## Project layout
155	
156	Prefer `pyproject.toml` over `requirements.txt` when using `uv`:
157	
158	```toml
159	[project]
160	name = "my-mcp-server"
161	version = "1.0.0"
162	requires-python = ">=3.11"
163	dependencies = [
164	    "fastmcp==3.0.2",
165	    "python-dotenv>=1.0",
166	    "my-api-library>=1.0",
167	]
168	```
169	
170	## Code patterns
171	
172	### Separate client from server
173	
174	Keep API logic in a dedicated client module:
175	
176	```
177	mcps/my-server/
178	    server.py          # FastMCP tools — thin wrappers
179	    my_client.py       # API client — auth, token caching, raw calls
180	```
181	
182	### Logging
183	
184	```python
185	from logging.handlers import RotatingFileHandler
186	
187	_log_dir = Path(__file__).parent / "logs"
188	_log_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
189	logging.basicConfig(
190	    level=logging.WARNING,
191	    handlers=[RotatingFileHandler(_log_dir / "server.log", maxBytes=5_000_000, backupCount=1)],
192	)
193	```
194	
195	### FastMCP instructions
196	
197	Guide AI behavior with the `instructions` parameter:
198	
199	```python
200	mcp = FastMCP(
201	    "MyServer",
202	    instructions="""When the user asks to..., always do X first.
203	Before executing any write operation, tell the user and STOP.""",
204	)
205	```
206	
207	### Token caching
208	
209	For OAuth-based APIs, cache tokens alongside the server code so users don't re-auth every restart:
210	
211	```python
212	cache_path = Path(__file__).parent / ".my_token_cache"
213	```
214	
215	## Testing
216	
217	Create a simple test script first to validate API access before building the full server.
218	
219	## Connecting to Claude Code
220	
221	Always register with **user scope** (`-s user`) so the server is available in every project. Use `uv run` + `fastmcp run` as the command:
222	
223	```bash
224	claude mcp add -s user <name> -- uv run --directory /path/to/mcps/my-server fastmcp run server.py
225	```
226	
227	Pick a **generic name** that matches what the user would naturally say. For example, register a Spotify server as `music` so "play a song" triggers the right tools:
228	
229	```bash
230	claude mcp add -s user music -- uv run --directory /path/to/mcps/spotify fastmcp run server.py
231	```
232	
233	For HTTP servers:
234	
235	```bash
236	claude mcp add -s user --transport http <name> http://localhost:PORT/mcp
237	```
238	
239	## Connecting to Droid
240	
241	For Droid, register local stdio MCP servers with `droid mcp add` and pass the full launch command as a quoted string:
242	
243	```bash
244	droid mcp add <name> "uv run --directory /path/to/mcps/my-server fastmcp run server.py"
245	```
246	
247	Pick a **generic name** that matches what the user would naturally say. For example, register a Spotify server as `music` so "play a song" triggers the right tools:
248	
249	```bash
250	droid mcp add music "uv run --directory /path/to/mcps/spotify fastmcp run server.py"
251	```
252	
253	For HTTP servers:
254	
255	```bash
256	droid mcp add <name> http://localhost:PORT/mcp --type http
257	```
258	
259	Droid stores user-level MCP registrations in `~/.factory/mcp.json` (Windows: `%USERPROFILE%\.factory\mcp.json`).
260

file.content:
# MCP Servers

How to build and configure MCP servers in this repo.

## What is an MCP server?

An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server exposes tools, resources, and prompts to AI clients like Claude Code, Claude Desktop, and Cursor. Each server lives in `mcps/` and provides a focused set of capabilities (Gmail, WhatsApp, Drive, etc.).

## Structure

```
mcps/
  my-server/
    server.py / mcp-server.js   # Required — the MCP server entry point
    README.md                   # Required — tools table, setup, usage
    requirements.txt / package.json
    icon_48x48.png              # Optional — icon for Claude Desktop
```

## Adding an icon for Claude Desktop

MCP servers can declare an icon that shows up in Claude Desktop's Connectors menu. The icon is served during the MCP initialization handshake via the `icons` field in `serverInfo`.

### Steps

#### 1. Create a 48x48 PNG

Resize your icon to 48x48 pixels and save it alongside your server code:

```python
from PIL import Image
img = Image.open("icon.png")
img = img.resize((48, 48), Image.LANCZOS)
img.save("icon_48x48.png")
```

#### 2. Add it to serverInfo

**TypeScript (MCP SDK):**

```js
import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { join } from "node:path";

// For Electron apps, use app.getAppPath() instead of __dirname
const ICON_B64 = readFileSync(join(app.getAppPath(), "icon_48x48.png")).toString("base64");

const server = new McpServer({
  name: "my-server",
  version: "1.0.0",
  icons: [{
    src: `data:image/png;base64,${ICON_B64}`,
    mimeType: "image/png",
    sizes: ["48x48"],
  }],
});
```

**Python (FastMCP):**

```python
import base64
from pathlib import Path

icon_b64 = base64.b64encode(Path("icon_48x48.png").read_bytes()).decode()

# Pass icons in the server metadata (check FastMCP docs for exact API)
```

#### 3. For Electron apps: include in the build

Add the icon file to the `files` array in `package.json` so electron-builder bundles it:

```json
{
  "build": {
    "files": [
      "mcp-server.js",
      "icon_48x48.png"
    ]
  }
}
```

Use `app.getAppPath()` (not `__dirname`) to resolve the file path — `__dirname` points to a different location in packaged Electron apps.

### Icon spec (MCP 2025-11-25)

| Field | Required | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| `src` | Yes | Data URI (`data:image/png;base64,...`) or HTTPS URL |
| `mimeType` | No | `image/png`, `image/jpeg`, `image/svg+xml`, `image/webp` |
| `sizes` | No | Array of size strings, e.g. `["48x48"]` |
| `theme` | No | `light` or `dark` |

Claude Desktop must support PNG and JPEG. SVG and WebP are optional.

## Connecting to Claude Desktop

Claude Desktop only supports **stdio** for local MCP servers. If your server runs over HTTP (like an Electron app), use a bridge:

### Direct stdio server

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "my-server": {
      "command": "uv",
      "args": ["run", "--directory", "/path/to/mcps/my-server", "fastmcp", "run", "server.py"]
    }
  }
}
```

### HTTP server via supergateway bridge

For servers that expose an HTTP endpoint (e.g. `http://localhost:PORT/mcp`), use [supergateway](https://github.com/supercorp-ai/supergateway) to bridge HTTP to stdio:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "my-server": {
      "command": "cmd",
      "args": ["/c", "npx", "-y", "supergateway", "--streamableHttp", "http://localhost:PORT/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

On macOS/Linux, drop the `"cmd", "/c"` prefix.

Config file location:
- **Windows:** `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json`
- **macOS:** `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`

## Credentials

Store secrets in the repo root `.env` (already gitignored). Servers load them with `python-dotenv` and pass explicitly via `os.getenv()`:

```python
import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from pathlib import Path

load_dotenv(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / ".env")

# Always pass credentials explicitly — don't rely on libraries reading env vars implicitly
client = SomeAPI(
    api_key=os.getenv("MY_API_KEY"),
    secret=os.getenv("MY_API_SECRET"),
)
```

## Project layout

Prefer `pyproject.toml` over `requirements.txt` when using `uv`:

```toml
[project]
name = "my-mcp-server"
version = "1.0.0"
requires-python = ">=3.11"
dependencies = [
    "fastmcp==3.0.2",
    "python-dotenv>=1.0",
    "my-api-library>=1.0",
]
```

## Code patterns

### Separate client from server

Keep API logic in a dedicated client module:

```
mcps/my-server/
    server.py          # FastMCP tools — thin wrappers
    my_client.py       # API client — auth, token caching, raw calls
```

### Logging

```python
from logging.handlers import RotatingFileHandler

_log_dir = Path(__file__).parent / "logs"
_log_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
logging.basicConfig(
    level=logging.WARNING,
    handlers=[RotatingFileHandler(_log_dir / "server.log", maxBytes=5_000_000, backupCount=1)],
)
```

### FastMCP instructions

Guide AI behavior with the `instructions` parameter:

```python
mcp = FastMCP(
    "MyServer",
    instructions="""When the user asks to..., always do X first.
Before executing any write operation, tell the user and STOP.""",
)
```

### Token caching

For OAuth-based APIs, cache tokens alongside the server code so users don't re-auth every restart:

```python
cache_path = Path(__file__).parent / ".my_token_cache"
```

## Testing

Create a simple test script first to validate API access before building the full server.

## Connecting to Claude Code

Always register with **user scope** (`-s user`) so the server is available in every project. Use `uv run` + `fastmcp run` as the command:

```bash
claude mcp add -s user <name> -- uv run --directory /path/to/mcps/my-server fastmcp run server.py
```

Pick a **generic name** that matches what the user would naturally say. For example, register a Spotify server as `music` so "play a song" triggers the right tools:

```bash
claude mcp add -s user music -- uv run --directory /path/to/mcps/spotify fastmcp run server.py
```

For HTTP servers:

```bash
claude mcp add -s user --transport http <name> http://localhost:PORT/mcp
```

## Connecting to Droid

For Droid, register local stdio MCP servers with `droid mcp add` and pass the full launch command as a quoted string:

```bash
droid mcp add <name> "uv run --directory /path/to/mcps/my-server fastmcp run server.py"
```

Pick a **generic name** that matches what the user would naturally say. For example, register a Spotify server as `music` so "play a song" triggers the right tools:

```bash
droid mcp add music "uv run --directory /path/to/mcps/spotify fastmcp run server.py"
```

For HTTP servers:

```bash
droid mcp add <name> http://localhost:PORT/mcp --type http
```

Droid stores user-level MCP registrations in `~/.factory/mcp.json` (Windows: `%USERPROFILE%\.factory\mcp.json`).
